     137                 :            : 
     138                 :            : /* ==========================================================================
     139                 :            :  * The follwing macros aid in major refactoring of data processing code (in
     140                 :            :  * CopyFrom(+Dispatch)). We use macros because in some cases the code must be in
     141                 :            :  * line in order to work (for example elog_dismiss() in PG_CATCH) while in
     142                 :            :  * other cases we'd like to inline the code for performance reasons.
     143                 :            :  *
     144                 :            :  * NOTE that an almost identical set of macros exists in fileam.c. If you make
     145                 :            :  * changes here you may want to consider taking a look there as well.
     146                 :            :  * ==========================================================================
     147                 :            :  */
     148                 :            : 
     149                 :            : #define RESET_LINEBUF \
     150                 :            : cstate->line_buf.len = 0; \
     151                 :            : cstate->line_buf.data[0] = '\0'; \
     152                 :            : cstate->line_buf.cursor = 0;
     153                 :            : 
     154                 :            : #define RESET_ATTRBUF \
     155                 :            : cstate->attribute_buf.len = 0; \
     156                 :            : cstate->attribute_buf.data[0] = '\0'; \
     157                 :            : cstate->attribute_buf.cursor = 0;
     158                 :            : 
     159                 :            : #define RESET_LINEBUF_WITH_LINENO \
     160                 :            : line_buf_with_lineno.len = 0; \
     161                 :            : line_buf_with_lineno.data[0] = '\0'; \
     162                 :            : line_buf_with_lineno.cursor = 0;
     163                 :            : 
     164                 :            : /*
     165                 :            :  * A data error happened. This code block will always be inside a PG_CATCH()
     166                 :            :  * block right when a higher stack level produced an error. We handle the error
     167                 :            :  * by checking which error mode is set (SREH or all-or-nothing) and do the right
     168                 :            :  * thing accordingly. Note that we MUST have this code in a macro (as opposed
     169                 :            :  * to a function) as elog_dismiss() has to be inlined with PG_CATCH in order to
     170                 :            :  * access local error state variables.
     171                 :            :  *
     172                 :            :  * changing me? take a look at FILEAM_HANDLE_ERROR in fileam.c as well.
     173                 :            :  */
     174                 :            : #define COPY_HANDLE_ERROR \
     175                 :            : if (cstate->errMode == ALL_OR_NOTHING) \
     176                 :            : { \
     177                 :            :         /* re-throw error and abort */ \
     178                 :            :         if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH) \
     179                 :            :                 cdbCopyEnd(cdbCopy); \
     180                 :            :         PG_RE_THROW(); \
     181                 :            : } \
     182                 :            : else \
     183                 :            : { \
     184                 :            :         /* SREH - release error state and handle error */ \
     185                 :            : \
     186                 :            :         ErrorData       *edata; \
     187                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcontext;\
     188                 :            :         bool    rawdata_is_a_copy = false; \
     189                 :            :         cur_row_rejected = true; \
     190                 :            : \
     191                 :            :         /* SREH must only handle data errors. all other errors must not be caught */\
     192                 :            :         if (ERRCODE_TO_CATEGORY(elog_geterrcode()) != ERRCODE_DATA_EXCEPTION)\
     193                 :            :         {\
     194                 :            :                 /* re-throw error and abort */ \
     195                 :            :                 if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH) \
     196                 :            :                         cdbCopyEnd(cdbCopy); \
     197                 :            :                 PG_RE_THROW(); \
     198                 :            :         }\
     199                 :            : \
     200                 :            :         /* save a copy of the error info */ \
     201                 :            :         oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(cstate->cdbsreh->badrowcontext);\
     202                 :            :         edata = CopyErrorData();\
     203                 :            :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);\
     204                 :            : \
     205                 :            :         if (!elog_dismiss(DEBUG5)) \
     206                 :            :                 PG_RE_THROW(); /* <-- hope to never get here! */ \
     207                 :            : \
     208                 :            :         if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH)\
     209                 :            :         {\
     210                 :            :                 Insist(cstate->err_loc_type == ROWNUM_ORIGINAL);\
     211                 :            :                 cstate->cdbsreh->rawdata = (char *) palloc(strlen(cstate->line_buf.data) * \
     212                 :            :                                                                                                    sizeof(char) + 1 + 24); \
     213                 :            : \
     214                 :            :                 rawdata_is_a_copy = true; \
     215                 :            :                 sprintf(cstate->cdbsreh->rawdata, "%d%c%d%c%s", \
     216                 :            :                             original_lineno_for_qe, \
     217                 :            :                                 COPY_METADATA_DELIM, \
     218                 :            :                                 cstate->line_buf_converted, \
     219                 :            :                                 COPY_METADATA_DELIM, \
     220                 :            :                                 cstate->line_buf.data);      \
     221                 :            :         }\
     222                 :            :         else\
     223                 :            :         {\
     224                 :            :                 if (Gp_role == GP_ROLE_EXECUTE)\
     225                 :            :                 {\
     226                 :            :                         /* if line has embedded rownum, update the cursor to the pos right after */ \
     227                 :            :                         Insist(cstate->err_loc_type == ROWNUM_EMBEDDED);\
     228                 :            :                         cstate->line_buf.cursor = 0;\
     229                 :            :                         if(!cstate->md_error) \
     230                 :            :                                 CopyExtractRowMetaData(cstate); \
     231                 :            :                 }\
     232                 :            : \
     233                 :            :                 cstate->cdbsreh->rawdata = cstate->line_buf.data + cstate->line_buf.cursor; \
     234                 :            :         }\
     235                 :            : \
     236                 :            :         cstate->cdbsreh->is_server_enc = cstate->line_buf_converted; \
     237                 :            :         cstate->cdbsreh->linenumber = cstate->cur_lineno; \
     238                 :            :         cstate->cdbsreh->processed = ++cstate->processed; \
     239                 :            :         cstate->cdbsreh->consec_csv_err = cstate->num_consec_csv_err; \
     240                 :            : \
     241                 :            :         /* set the error message. Use original msg and add column name if available */ \
     242                 :            :         if (cstate->cur_attname)\
     243                 :            :         {\
     244                 :            :                 cstate->cdbsreh->errmsg = (char *) palloc((strlen(edata->message) + \
     245                 :            :                                                                                                   strlen(cstate->cur_attname) + \
     246                 :            :                                                                                                   10 + 1) * sizeof(char)); \
     247                 :            :                 sprintf(cstate->cdbsreh->errmsg, "%s, column %s", \
     248                 :            :                                 edata->message, \
     249                 :            :                                 cstate->cur_attname); \
     250                 :            :         }\
     251                 :            :         else\
     252                 :            :         {\
     253                 :            :                 cstate->cdbsreh->errmsg = pstrdup(edata->message); \
     254                 :            :         }\
     255                 :            : \
     256                 :            :         /* after all the prep work let cdbsreh do the real work */ \
     257                 :            :         HandleSingleRowError(cstate->cdbsreh); \
     258                 :            : \
     259                 :            :         /* cleanup any extra memory copies we made */\
     260                 :            :         if (rawdata_is_a_copy) \
     261                 :            :                 pfree(cstate->cdbsreh->rawdata); \
     262                 :            :         if (!IsRejectLimitReached(cstate->cdbsreh)) \
     263                 :            :                 pfree(cstate->cdbsreh->errmsg); \
     264                 :            : \
     265                 :            :         MemoryContextReset(cstate->cdbsreh->badrowcontext);\
     266                 :            : \
     267                 :            : }
     268                 :            : 
     269                 :            : /*
     270                 :            :  * if in SREH mode and data error occured it was already handled in
     271                 :            :  * COPY_HANDLE_ERROR. Therefore, skip to the next row before attempting
     272                 :            :  * to do any further processing on this one. There's a QE and QD versions
     273                 :            :  * since the QE doesn't have a linebuf_with_lineno stringInfo.
     274                 :            :  */
     275                 :            : #define QD_GOTO_NEXT_ROW \
     276                 :            : RESET_LINEBUF_WITH_LINENO; \
     277                 :            : RESET_LINEBUF; \
     278                 :            : cur_row_rejected = false; /* reset for next run */ \
     279                 :            : continue; /* move on to the next data line */
     280                 :            : 
     281                 :            : #define QE_GOTO_NEXT_ROW \
     282                 :            : RESET_LINEBUF; \
     283                 :            : cur_row_rejected = false; /* reset for next run */ \
     284                 :            : cstate->cur_attname = NULL;\
     285                 :            : continue; /* move on to the next data line */
     286                 :            :

     288                 :            :  * Send copy start/stop messages for frontend copies.  These have changed

     290                 :            :  */

     316                 :            :                         errmsg("COPY BINARY is not supported to stdout or from stdin")));

     382                 :            : {

     391                 :            :         {

     398                 :            : 
     399                 :            : /*----------
     400                 :            :  * CopySendData sends output data to the destination (file or frontend)
     401                 :            :  * CopySendString does the same for null-terminated strings
     402                 :            :  * CopySendChar does the same for single characters
     403                 :            :  * CopySendEndOfRow does the appropriate thing at end of each data row
     404                 :            :  *      (data is not actually flushed except by CopySendEndOfRow)
     405                 :            :  *
     406                 :            :  * NB: no data conversion is applied by these functions
     407                 :            :  *----------
     408                 :            :  */
     409                 :            : static void
     410                 :            : CopySendData(CopyState cstate, const void *databuf, int datasize)
     411                 :            : {
     412         [ +  + ]:     108700 :         if (!cstate->is_copy_in) /* copy out */
     413                 :            :         {
     414                 :     108672 :                 appendBinaryStringInfo(cstate->fe_msgbuf, (char *) databuf, datasize);
     415                 :     108672 :         }
     416                 :            :         else /* hack for: copy in */
     417                 :            :         {
     418                 :            :                 /* we call copySendData in copy-in to handle results
     419                 :            :                  * of default functions that we wish to send from the
     420                 :            :                  * dispatcher to the executor primary and mirror segments.
     421                 :            :                  * we do so by concatenating the results to line buffer.
     422                 :            :                  */
     423                 :         28 :                 appendBinaryStringInfo(&cstate->line_buf, (char *) databuf, datasize);
     424                 :            :         }
     425                 :     108700 : }
     426                 :            : 
     427                 :            : static void
     428                 :            : CopySendString(CopyState cstate, const char *str)
     429                 :            : {

     438                 :            : 
     439                 :            : /* AXG: Note that this will both add a newline AND flush the data.
     440                 :            :  * For the dispatcher COPY TO we don't want to use this method since
     441                 :            :  * our newlines already exist. We use another new method similar to
     442                 :            :  * this one to flush the data
     443                 :            :  */

     453                 :            :                         {

     460                 :       1031 :                         }
     461                 :            :

     467                 :            :                                                  errmsg("could not write to COPY file: %m")));
     468                 :            : 
     469                 :            :                         /* Send "\n" to QD for "processed" line number counting */

     471                 :            :                         {

     477                 :       1024 :                         }
     478                 :            : 
     479                 :       1041 :                         break;

     481                 :            :                         /* The FE/BE protocol uses \n as newline for all platforms */

     483                 :          0 :                                 CopySendChar(cstate, '\n');
     484                 :            : 
     485         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (pq_putbytes(fe_msgbuf->data, fe_msgbuf->len))
     486                 :            :                         {

     490                 :            :                                                  errmsg("connection lost during COPY to stdout")));
     491                 :          0 :                         }
     492                 :          0 :                         break;
     493                 :            :                 case COPY_NEW_FE:
     494                 :            :                         /* The FE/BE protocol uses \n as newline for all platforms */

     497                 :            :

     500                 :       2110 :                         break;

     505                 :            : #else

     560                 :            : /*
     561                 :            :  * CopyGetData reads data from the source (file or frontend)
     562                 :            :  * CopyGetChar does the same for single characters
     563                 :            :  *
     564                 :            :  * CopyGetEof checks if EOF was detected by previous Get operation.
     565                 :            :  *
     566                 :            :  * Note: when copying from the frontend, we expect a proper EOF mark per
     567                 :            :  * protocol; if the frontend simply drops the connection, we raise error.
     568                 :            :  * It seems unwise to allow the COPY IN to complete normally in that case.
     569                 :            :  *
     570                 :            :  * NB: no data conversion is applied by these functions
     571                 :            :  *
     572                 :            :  * Returns: the number of bytes that were successfully read
     573                 :            :  * into the data buffer.
     574                 :            :  */
     575                 :            : static int
     576                 :            : CopyGetData(CopyState cstate, void *databuf, int datasize)
     577                 :            : {
     578                 :       5251 :         size_t          bytesread = 0;
     579                 :            : 
     580   [ +  -  +  -  :       5251 :         switch (cstate->copy_dest)
                      + ]
     581                 :            :         {
     582                 :            :                 case COPY_FILE:
     583                 :        385 :                         bytesread = fread(databuf, 1, datasize, cstate->copy_file);
     584         [ +  + ]:        385 :                         if (feof(cstate->copy_file))
     585                 :        187 :                                 cstate->fe_eof = true;
     586                 :        385 :                         break;
     587                 :            :                 case COPY_OLD_FE:
     588         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (pq_getbytes((char *) databuf, datasize))
     589                 :            :                         {
     590                 :            :                                 /* Only a \. terminator is legal EOF in old protocol */
     591         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
     592                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),
     593                 :            :                                                  errmsg("unexpected EOF on client connection")));
     594                 :          0 :                         }
     595                 :          0 :                         bytesread += datasize;          /* update the count of bytes that were
     596                 :            :                                                                                  * read so far */
     597                 :          0 :                         break;
     598                 :            :                 case COPY_NEW_FE:
     599 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     960197 :                         while (datasize > 0 && !cstate->fe_eof)
     600                 :            :                         {
     601                 :            :                                 int                     avail;
     602                 :            : 
     603         [ +  + ]:    1913554 :                                 while (cstate->fe_msgbuf->cursor >= cstate->fe_msgbuf->len)
     604                 :            :                                 {
     605                 :            :                                         /* Try to receive another message */
     606                 :     956847 :                                         int                     mtype;
     607                 :            : 
     608                 :            :                         readmessage:
     609                 :     956847 :                                         mtype = pq_getbyte();
     610         [ -  + ]:     956847 :                                         if (mtype == EOF)
     611         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
     612                 :            :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),
     613                 :            :                                                          errmsg("unexpected EOF on client connection")));
     614         [ -  + ]:     956847 :                                         if (pq_getmessage(cstate->fe_msgbuf, 0))
     615         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
     616                 :            :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_FAILURE),
     617                 :            :                                                          errmsg("unexpected EOF on client connection")));
     618   [ +  +  -  -  :     956847 :                                         switch (mtype)
                   -  - ]
     619                 :            :                                         {
     620                 :            :                                                 case 'd':               /* CopyData */
     621                 :     955523 :                                                         break;
     622                 :            :                                                 case 'c':               /* CopyDone */
     623                 :            :                                                         /* COPY IN correctly terminated by frontend */
     624                 :       1324 :                                                         cstate->fe_eof = true;
     625                 :       1324 :                                                         return bytesread;
     626                 :            :                                                 case 'f':               /* CopyFail */
     627         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
     628                 :            :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_QUERY_CANCELED),
     629                 :            :                                                                          errmsg("COPY from stdin failed: %s",
     630                 :            :                                                                            pq_getmsgstring(cstate->fe_msgbuf))));
     631                 :          0 :                                                         break;
     632                 :            :                                                 case 'H':               /* Flush */
     633                 :            :                                                 case 'S':               /* Sync */
     634                 :            : 
     635                 :            :                                                         /*
     636                 :            :                                                          * Ignore Flush/Sync for the convenience of client
     637                 :            :                                                          * libraries (such as libpq) that may send those
     638                 :            :                                                          * without noticing that the command they just
     639                 :            :                                                          * sent was COPY.
     640                 :            :                                                          */

     642                 :            :                                                 default:
     643         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
     644                 :            :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_PROTOCOL_VIOLATION),
     645                 :            :                                                                          errmsg("unexpected message type 0x%02X during COPY from stdin",

     648                 :            :                                         }
     649                 :            :                                 }

     653                 :     956707 :                                 pq_copymsgbytes(cstate->fe_msgbuf, databuf, avail);
     654                 :     956707 :                                 databuf = (void *) ((char *) databuf + avail);
     655                 :     956707 :                                 bytesread += avail;             /* update the count of bytes that were
     656                 :            :                                                                                  * read so far */

     658                 :            :                         }
     659                 :       2166 :                         break;

     662                 :          0 :                         break;
     663                 :            : 
     664                 :            :         }

     669                 :            : /*
     670                 :            :  * These functions do apply some data conversion
     671                 :            :  */
     672                 :            : 
     673                 :            : /*
     674                 :            :  * CopySendInt32 sends an int32 in network byte order
     675                 :            :  */
     676                 :            : static void

     687                 :            :  *
     688                 :            :  * Returns true if OK, false if EOF

     699                 :            :         }

     701                 :         50 :         return true;
     702                 :         50 : }
     703                 :            :

     706                 :            :  *
     707                 :            :  * Returns true if OK, false if EOF
     708                 :            :  */

     710                 :            : CopyGetInt64(CopyState cstate, int64 *val)
     711                 :            : {

     752                 :            : 
     753                 :            : /*
     754                 :            :  * ValidateControlChars
     755                 :            :  *
     756                 :            :  * These routine is common for COPY and external tables. It validates the
     757                 :            :  * control characters (delimiter, quote, etc..) and enforces the given rules.
     758                 :            :  *
     759                 :            :  * bool copy
     760                 :            :  *  - pass true if you're COPY
     761                 :            :  *  - pass false if you're an exttab
     762                 :            :  *
     763                 :            :  * bool load
     764                 :            :  *  - pass true for inbound data (COPY FROM, SELECT FROM exttab)
     765                 :            :  *  - pass false for outbound data (COPY TO, INSERT INTO exttab)
     766                 :            :  */
     767                 :            : void ValidateControlChars(bool copy, bool load, bool csv_mode, char *delim,
     768                 :            :                                                 char *null_print, char *quote, char *escape,
     769                 :            :                                                 List *force_quote, List *force_notnull,
     770                 :            :                                                 bool header_line, bool fill_missing, char *newline,
     771                 :            :                                                 int num_columns)
     772                 :            : {
     773                 :       2136 :         bool    delim_off = (pg_strcasecmp(delim, "off") == 0);
     774                 :            : 
     775                 :            :         /*
     776                 :            :          * DELIMITER
     777                 :            :          *
     778                 :            :          * Only single-byte delimiter strings are supported. In addition, if the
     779                 :            :          * server encoding is a multibyte character encoding we only allow the
     780                 :            :          * delimiter to be an ASCII character (like postgresql. For more info
     781                 :            :          * on this see discussion and comments in MPP-3756).
     782                 :            :          */
     783         [ -  + ]:       2136 :         if (pg_database_encoding_max_length() == 1)
     784                 :            :         {
     785                 :            :                 /* single byte encoding such as ascii, latinx and other */
     786 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (strlen(delim) != 1 && !delim_off)
     787         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     788                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
     789                 :            :                                         errmsg("delimiter must be a single byte character, or \'off\'")));
     790                 :          0 :         }
     791                 :            :         else
     792                 :            :         {
     793                 :            :                 /* multi byte encoding such as utf8 */
     794 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       2136 :                 if ((strlen(delim) != 1 || IS_HIGHBIT_SET(delim[0])) && !delim_off )
                 [ #  # ]
     795         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     796                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
     797                 :            :                                         errmsg("delimiter must be a single ASCII character, or \'off\'")));
     798                 :            :         }
     799                 :            : 
     800 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       2136 :         if (strchr(delim, '\r') != NULL ||
     801                 :       2136 :                 strchr(delim, '\n') != NULL)
     802         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     803                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
     804                 :            :                                 errmsg("delimiter cannot be newline or carriage return")));
     805                 :            : 
     806 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       2136 :         if (strchr(null_print, '\r') != NULL ||
     807                 :       2136 :                 strchr(null_print, '\n') != NULL)
     808         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     809                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
     810                 :            :                                 errmsg("null representation cannot use newline or carriage return")));
     811                 :            : 
     812 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:       2136 :         if (!csv_mode && strchr(delim, '\\') != NULL)
     813         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     814                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
     815                 :            :                                 errmsg("delimiter cannot be backslash")));
     816                 :            : 
     817 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:       2136 :         if (strchr(null_print, delim[0]) != NULL && !delim_off)
     818         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     819                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
     820                 :            :                 errmsg("delimiter must not appear in the NULL specification")));
     821                 :            : 
     822                 :            :         /*
     823                 :            :          * Disallow unsafe delimiter characters in non-CSV mode.  We can't allow
     824                 :            :          * backslash because it would be ambiguous.  We can't allow the other
     825                 :            :          * cases because data characters matching the delimiter must be
     826                 :            :          * backslashed, and certain backslash combinations are interpreted
     827                 :            :          * non-literally by COPY IN.  Disallowing all lower case ASCII letters
     828                 :            :          * is more than strictly necessary, but seems best for consistency and
     829                 :            :          * future-proofing.  Likewise we disallow all digits though only octal
     830                 :            :          * digits are actually dangerous.
     831                 :            :          */
     832 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:       2136 :         if (!csv_mode && !delim_off &&
                 [ -  + ]
     833                 :       1835 :                 strchr("\\.abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789", delim[0]) != NULL)
     834         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     835                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
     836                 :            :                                 errmsg("delimiter cannot be \"%s\"", delim)));
     837                 :            : 
     838         [ -  + ]:       2136 :         if (delim_off)
     839                 :            :         {
     840                 :            : 
     841                 :            :                 /*
     842                 :            :                  * We don't support delimiter 'off' for COPY because the QD COPY
     843                 :            :                  * sometimes internally adds columns to the data that it sends to
     844                 :            :                  * the QE COPY modules, and it uses the delimiter for it. There
     845                 :            :                  * are ways to work around this but for now it's not important and
     846                 :            :                  * we simply don't support it.
     847                 :            :                  */
     848         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (copy)
     849         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     850                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
     851                 :            :                                         errmsg("Using no delimiter is only supported for external tables")));
     852                 :            : 
     853         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (num_columns != 1)
     854         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
     855                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
     856                 :            :                                         errmsg("Using no delimiter is only possible for a single column table")));
     857                 :            : 
     858                 :          0 :         }
     859                 :            : 
     860                 :            :         /*
     861                 :            :          * HEADER
     862                 :            :          */
     863         [ +  + ]:       2136 :         if(header_line)
     864                 :            :         {
     865 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:          9 :                 if(!copy && Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH)
     866                 :            :                 {
     867                 :            :                         /* (exttab) */
     868         [ +  - ]:          1 :                         if(load)
     869                 :            :                         {
     870                 :            :                                 /* RET */
     871         [ +  - ]:          1 :                                 ereport(NOTICE,
     872                 :            :                                                 (errmsg("HEADER means that each one of the data files "
     873                 :            :                                                                 "has a header row.")));                               
     874                 :          1 :                         }
     875                 :            :                         else
     876                 :            :                         {
     877                 :            :                                 /* WET */
     878         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
     879                 :            :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_GP_FEATURE_NOT_YET),
     880                 :            :                                                 errmsg("HEADER is not yet supported for writable external tables")));                         
     881                 :            :                         }
     882                 :          1 :                 }

     983                 :            : 
     984                 :            : 
     985                 :            : /*
     986                 :            :  *       DoCopy executes the SQL COPY statement
     987                 :            :  *
     988                 :            :  * Either unload or reload contents of table <relation>, depending on <from>.
     989                 :            :  * (<from> = TRUE means we are inserting into the table.) In the "TO" case
     990                 :            :  * we also support copying the output of an arbitrary SELECT query.
     991                 :            :  *
     992                 :            :  * If <pipe> is false, transfer is between the table and the file named
     993                 :            :  * <filename>.    Otherwise, transfer is between the table and our regular
     994                 :            :  * input/output stream. The latter could be either stdin/stdout or a
     995                 :            :  * socket, depending on whether we're running under Postmaster control.
     996                 :            :  *
     997                 :            :  * Iff <binary>, unload or reload in the binary format, as opposed to the
     998                 :            :  * more wasteful but more robust and portable text format.
     999                 :            :  *
    1000                 :            :  * Iff <oids>, unload or reload the format that includes OID information.
    1001                 :            :  * On input, we accept OIDs whether or not the table has an OID column,
    1002                 :            :  * but silently drop them if it does not.  On output, we report an error
    1003                 :            :  * if the user asks for OIDs in a table that has none (not providing an
    1004                 :            :  * OID column might seem friendlier, but could seriously confuse programs).
    1005                 :            :  *
    1006                 :            :  * If in the text format, delimit columns with delimiter <delim> and print
    1007                 :            :  * NULL values as <null_print>.
    1008                 :            :  *
    1009                 :            :  * When loading in the text format from an input stream (as opposed to
    1010                 :            :  * a file), recognize a "\." on a line by itself as EOF. Also recognize
    1011                 :            :  * a stream EOF.  When unloading in the text format to an output stream,
    1012                 :            :  * write a "." on a line by itself at the end of the data.
    1013                 :            :  *
    1014                 :            :  * Do not allow a Postgres user without superuser privilege to read from
    1015                 :            :  * or write to a file.
    1016                 :            :  *
    1017                 :            :  * Do not allow the copy if user doesn't have proper permission to access
    1018                 :            :  * the table.
    1019                 :            :  */
    1020                 :            : uint64
    1021                 :            : DoCopyInternal(const CopyStmt *stmt, const char *queryString, CopyState cstate)
    1022                 :            : {
    1023                 :       1595 :         bool            is_from = stmt->is_from;
    1024         [ +  + ]:       1595 :         bool            pipe = (stmt->filename == NULL || Gp_role == GP_ROLE_EXECUTE);

    1027                 :       1595 :         List       *force_notnull = NIL;
    1028                 :       1595 :         AclMode         required_access = (is_from ? ACL_INSERT : ACL_SELECT);

    1030                 :            :         ListCell   *option;

    1037                 :            :

    1044                 :            :                 {

    1046         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,

    1653                 :            : 
    1654                 :            :                 /*
    1655                 :            :                  * Append Only Tables.
    1656                 :            :                  *
    1657                 :            :                  * If QD, build a list of all the relations (relids) that may get data
    1658                 :            :                  * inserted into them as a part of this operation. This includes
    1659                 :            :                  * the relation specified in the COPY command, plus any partitions
    1660                 :            :                  * that it may have. Then, call assignPerRelSegno to assign a segfile
    1661                 :            :                  * number to insert into each of the Append Only relations that exists
    1662                 :            :                  * in this global list. We generate the list now and save it in cstate.
    1663                 :            :                  *
    1664                 :            :                  * If QE - get the QD generated list from CopyStmt and each relation can
    1665                 :            :                  * find it's assigned segno by looking at it (during CopyFrom).
    1666                 :            :                  *
    1667                 :            :                  * Utility mode always builds a one single mapping.
    1668                 :            :                  */
    1669         [ +  + ]:       1420 :                 if(shouldDispatch)
    1670                 :            :                 {
    1671                 :        355 :                         Oid relid = RelationGetRelid(cstate->rel);
    1672                 :        355 :                         List *all_relids = NIL;
    1673                 :            : 
    1674                 :        355 :                         all_relids = lappend_oid(all_relids, relid);
    1675                 :            : 
    1676         [ +  + ]:        355 :                         if (rel_is_partitioned(relid))
    1677                 :            :                         {
    1678                 :         14 :                                 PartitionNode *pn = RelationBuildPartitionDesc(cstate->rel, false);
    1679                 :         14 :                                 all_relids = list_concat(all_relids, all_partition_relids(pn));
    1680                 :         14 :                         }
    1681                 :            : 
    1682                 :        355 :                         cstate->ao_segnos = assignPerRelSegno(all_relids);
    1683                 :        355 :                 }

    1929                 :            : /*
    1930                 :            :  * CopyToCreateDispatchCommand
    1931                 :            :  *
    1932                 :            :  * Create the COPY command that will get dispatched to the QE's.

    1934                 :            : static void CopyToCreateDispatchCommand(CopyState cstate,
    1935                 :            :                                                                                 StringInfo cdbcopy_cmd,
    1936                 :            :                                                                                 AttrNumber      num_phys_attrs,
    1937                 :            :                                                                                 Form_pg_attribute *attr)
    1938                 :            : 
    1939                 :            : {

    1947                 :         37 :                                          quote_identifier(RelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel)));

    1950                 :            :          * include all non-dropped columns of the table by default
    1951                 :            :          */

    1954         [ +  + ]:        181 :                 foreach(cur, cstate->attnumlist)
    1955                 :            :                 {

    1958                 :            : 
    1959                 :            :                         /* We don't add dropped attributes */
    1960         [ -  + ]:        146 :                         if (attr[m]->attisdropped)
    1961                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    1962                 :            : 
    1963                 :            :                         /* append column string. quote it if needed */
    1964                 :        146 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, (is_first_col ? "(%s" : ",%s"),
    1965                 :        146 :                                                          quote_identifier(NameStr(attr[m]->attname)));
    1966                 :            : 
    1967                 :        146 :                         is_first_col = false;
    1968                 :        146 :                 }
    1969                 :            : 
    1970         [ +  - ]:         35 :                 if (!is_first_col)
    1971                 :         35 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, ")");

    1973                 :            : 
    1974         [ +  + ]:         37 :         if (cstate->on_segment)
    1975                 :            :         {
    1976                 :          6 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " TO '%s' WITH ON SEGMENT", cstate->filename);
    1977                 :          6 :         }
    1978                 :            :         else
    1979                 :            :         {
    1980                 :         31 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " TO STDOUT WITH");
    1981                 :            :         }
    1982                 :            : 
    1983         [ +  + ]:         37 :         if (cstate->oids)
    1984                 :          1 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " OIDS");
    1985                 :            : 
    1986         [ +  + ]:         37 :         if (cstate->binary)
    1987                 :          2 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " BINARY");
    1988                 :            :         else
    1989                 :            :         {
    1990                 :         35 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " DELIMITER AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->delim));
    1991                 :         35 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " NULL AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->null_print));
    1992                 :            : 
    1993                 :            :                 /* if default escape in text format ("\") leave expression out */

    1996                 :            : 
    1997         [ +  + ]:         35 :                 if (cstate->csv_mode)
    1998                 :            :                 {

    2009                 :         18 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " ESCAPE AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->escape));

    2032                 :            : 
    2033                 :            : 
    2034                 :            : /*
    2035                 :            :  * Copy from relation TO file. Starts a COPY TO command on each of
    2036                 :            :  * the executors and gathers all the results and writes it out.
    2037                 :            :  */
    2038                 :            :  void
    2039                 :            : CopyToDispatch(CopyState cstate)
    2040                 :            : {
    2041                 :            :         TupleDesc       tupDesc;
    2042                 :            :         int                     num_phys_attrs;
    2043                 :            :         int                     attr_count;
    2044                 :            :         Form_pg_attribute *attr;
    2045                 :            :         CdbCopy    *cdbCopy;
    2046                 :            :         StringInfoData cdbcopy_err;
    2047                 :            :         StringInfoData cdbcopy_cmd;
    2048                 :            : 
    2049                 :         37 :         tupDesc = cstate->rel->rd_att;
    2050                 :         37 :         attr = tupDesc->attrs;
    2051                 :         37 :         num_phys_attrs = tupDesc->natts;
    2052                 :         37 :         attr_count = list_length(cstate->attnumlist);
    2053                 :            : 
    2054                 :            :         /* We use fe_msgbuf as a per-row buffer regardless of copy_dest */
    2055                 :         37 :         cstate->fe_msgbuf = makeStringInfo();
    2056                 :            : 
    2057                 :            :         /*
    2058                 :            :          * prepare to get COPY data from segDBs:
    2059                 :            :          * 1 - re-construct the orignial COPY command sent from the client.
    2060                 :            :          * 2 - execute a BEGIN DTM transaction.
    2061                 :            :          * 3 - send the COPY command to all segment databases.
    2062                 :            :          */
    2063                 :            : 
    2064                 :         37 :         cdbCopy = makeCdbCopy(false);
    2065                 :            : 
    2066                 :         37 :         cdbCopy->partitions = RelationBuildPartitionDesc(cstate->rel, false);
    2067                 :         37 :         cdbCopy->skip_ext_partition = cstate->skip_ext_partition;
    2068                 :            : 
    2069                 :            :         /* XXX: lock all partitions */
    2070                 :            : 
    2071                 :            :         /* allocate memory for error and copy strings */
    2072                 :         37 :         initStringInfo(&cdbcopy_err);
    2073                 :         37 :         initStringInfo(&cdbcopy_cmd);
    2074                 :            : 
    2075                 :            :         /* create the command to send to QE's and store it in cdbcopy_cmd */
    2076                 :         37 :         CopyToCreateDispatchCommand(cstate,
    2077                 :            :                                                                 &cdbcopy_cmd,
    2078                 :         37 :                                                                 num_phys_attrs,
    2079                 :         37 :                                                                 attr);
    2080                 :            : 
    2081                 :            :         /*
    2082                 :            :          * Start a COPY command in every db of every segment in Greenplum Database.
    2083                 :            :          *
    2084                 :            :          * From this point in the code we need to be extra careful
    2085                 :            :          * about error handling. ereport() must not be called until
    2086                 :            :          * the COPY command sessions are closed on the executors.
    2087                 :            :          * Calling ereport() will leave the executors hanging in
    2088                 :            :          * COPY state.
    2089                 :            :          */
    2090                 :         37 :         elog(DEBUG5, "COPY command sent to segdbs: %s", cdbcopy_cmd.data);
    2091                 :            : 
    2092         [ +  + ]:         38 :         PG_TRY();
    2093                 :            :         {
    2094                 :         37 :                 cdbCopyStart(cdbCopy, cdbcopy_cmd.data);
    2095                 :            :         }
    2096                 :         37 :         PG_CATCH();
    2097                 :            :         {
    2098                 :            :                 /* get error message from CopyStart */
    2099                 :          1 :                 appendBinaryStringInfo(&cdbcopy_err, cdbCopy->err_msg.data, cdbCopy->err_msg.len);
    2100                 :            : 
    2101                 :            :                 /* TODO: end COPY in all the segdbs in progress */
    2102                 :          1 :                 cdbCopyEnd(cdbCopy);
    2103                 :            : 
    2104         [ +  - ]:          1 :                 ereport(LOG,
    2105                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_CDB_INTERNAL_ERROR),
    2106                 :            :                                  errmsg("%s", cdbcopy_err.data)));
    2107                 :          1 :                 PG_RE_THROW();
    2108                 :            :         }
    2109                 :         36 :         PG_END_TRY();
    2110                 :            : 
    2111         [ +  + ]:         36 :         if (cstate->binary)
    2112                 :            :         {
    2113                 :            :                 /* Generate header for a binary copy */
    2114                 :            :                 int32           tmp;
    2115                 :            : 
    2116                 :            :                 /* Signature */

    2544                 :            : void
    2545                 :            : CopyOneCustomRowTo(CopyState cstate, bytea *value)
    2546                 :            : {
    2547                 :          0 :         appendBinaryStringInfo(cstate->fe_msgbuf,
    2548         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                    VARDATA_ANY((void *) value),
    2549 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                                    VARSIZE_ANY_EXHDR((void *) value));
    2550                 :          0 : }

    2552                 :            : /*
    2553                 :            :  * Emit one row during CopyTo().
    2554                 :            :  */
    2555                 :            : void
    2556                 :            : CopyOneRowTo(CopyState cstate, Oid tupleOid, Datum *values, bool *nulls)
    2557                 :            : {
    2558                 :       5958 :         bool            need_delim = false;
    2559                 :       5958 :         FmgrInfo   *out_functions = cstate->out_functions;
    2560                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcontext;
    2561                 :            :         ListCell   *cur;
    2562                 :            :         char       *string;
    2563                 :            : 
    2564                 :       5958 :         MemoryContextReset(cstate->rowcontext);
    2565                 :       5958 :         oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(cstate->rowcontext);
    2566                 :            : 
    2567         [ +  + ]:       5958 :         if (cstate->binary)
    2568                 :            :         {
    2569                 :            :                 /* Binary per-tuple header */
    2570                 :          8 :                 CopySendInt16(cstate, list_length(cstate->attnumlist));
    2571                 :            :                 /* Send OID if wanted --- note attnumlist doesn't include it */
    2572         [ -  + ]:          8 :                 if (cstate->oids)
    2573                 :            :                 {
    2574                 :            :                         /* Hack --- assume Oid is same size as int32 */
    2575                 :          0 :                         CopySendInt32(cstate, sizeof(int32));
    2576                 :          0 :                         CopySendInt32(cstate, tupleOid);
    2577                 :          0 :                 }
    2578                 :          8 :         }
    2579                 :            :         else
    2580                 :            :         {
    2581                 :            :                 /* Text format has no per-tuple header, but send OID if wanted */
    2582                 :            :                 /* Assume digits don't need any quoting or encoding conversion */
    2583         [ +  + ]:       5950 :                 if (cstate->oids)
    2584                 :            :                 {
    2585                 :          3 :                         string = DatumGetCString(DirectFunctionCall1(oidout,
    2586                 :          3 :                                                                                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(tupleOid)));
    2587                 :          3 :                         CopySendString(cstate, string);
    2588                 :          3 :                         need_delim = true;
    2589                 :          3 :                 }
    2590                 :            :         }
    2591                 :            : 
    2592         [ +  + ]:      59915 :         foreach(cur, cstate->attnumlist)
    2593                 :            :         {
    2594                 :      53957 :                 int                     attnum = lfirst_int(cur);
    2595                 :      53957 :                 Datum           value = values[attnum - 1];
    2596                 :      53957 :                 bool            isnull = nulls[attnum - 1];
    2597                 :            : 
    2598         [ +  + ]:      53957 :                 if (!cstate->binary)
    2599                 :            :                 {
    2600         [ +  + ]:      53921 :                         if (need_delim)
    2601                 :      47974 :                                 CopySendChar(cstate, cstate->delim[0]);
    2602                 :      53921 :                         need_delim = true;
    2603                 :      53921 :                 }
    2604                 :            : 
    2605         [ +  + ]:      53957 :                 if (isnull)
    2606                 :            :                 {
    2607         [ +  + ]:         18 :                         if (!cstate->binary)
    2608                 :         13 :                                 CopySendString(cstate, cstate->null_print_client);
    2609                 :            :                         else
    2610                 :          5 :                                 CopySendInt32(cstate, -1);
    2611                 :         18 :                 }
    2612                 :            :                 else
    2613                 :            :                 {
    2614         [ +  + ]:      53939 :                         if (!cstate->binary)
    2615                 :            :                         {
    2616         [ +  + ]:      53908 :                                 char            quotec = cstate->quote ? cstate->quote[0] : '\0';
    2617                 :            : 
    2618                 :            :                                 /* int2out or int4out ? */
    2619 [ +  - ][ +  + ]:      53908 :                                 if (out_functions[attnum -1].fn_oid == 39 ||  /* int2out or int4out */
    2620                 :      53908 :                                         out_functions[attnum -1].fn_oid == 43 )
    2621                 :            :                                 {
    2622                 :            :                                         char tmp[33];
    2623                 :            :                                         /*
    2624                 :            :                                          * The standard postgres way is to call the output function, but that involves one or more pallocs,
    2625                 :            :                                          * and a call to sprintf, followed by a conversion to client charset.
    2626                 :            :                                          * Do a fast conversion to string instead.
    2627                 :            :                                          */
    2628                 :            : 
    2629         [ -  + ]:      41918 :                                         if (out_functions[attnum -1].fn_oid ==  39)
    2630                 :          0 :                                                 pg_itoa(DatumGetInt16(value),tmp);
    2631                 :            :                                         else
    2632                 :      41918 :                                                 pg_ltoa(DatumGetInt32(value),tmp);
    2633                 :            : 
    2634                 :            :                                         /*
    2635                 :            :                                          * Integers don't need quoting, or transcoding to client char
    2636                 :            :                                          * set. We still quote them if FORCE QUOTE was used, though.
    2637                 :            :                                          */
    2638         [ +  + ]:      41918 :                                         if (cstate->force_quote_flags[attnum - 1])
    2639                 :         16 :                                                 CopySendChar(cstate, quotec);
    2640                 :      41918 :                                         CopySendData(cstate, tmp, strlen(tmp));
    2641         [ +  + ]:      41918 :                                         if (cstate->force_quote_flags[attnum - 1])
    2642                 :         16 :                                                 CopySendChar(cstate, quotec);
    2643                 :      41918 :                                 }
    2644         [ +  + ]:      11990 :                                 else if (out_functions[attnum -1].fn_oid == 1702)   /* numeric_out */
    2645                 :            :                                 {
    2646                 :          2 :                                         string = OutputFunctionCall(&out_functions[attnum - 1],
    2647                 :          2 :                                                                                                 value);
    2648                 :            :                                         /*
    2649                 :            :                                          * Numerics don't need quoting, or transcoding to client char
    2650                 :            :                                          * set. We still quote them if FORCE QUOTE was used, though.
    2651                 :            :                                          */
    2652         [ +  - ]:          2 :                                         if (cstate->force_quote_flags[attnum - 1])
    2653                 :          2 :                                                 CopySendChar(cstate, quotec);
    2654                 :          2 :                                         CopySendData(cstate, string, strlen(string));
    2655         [ +  - ]:          2 :                                         if (cstate->force_quote_flags[attnum - 1])
    2656                 :          2 :                                                 CopySendChar(cstate, quotec);
    2657                 :          2 :                                 }
    2658                 :            :                                 else
    2659                 :            :                                 {
    2660                 :      11988 :                                         string = OutputFunctionCall(&out_functions[attnum - 1],
    2661                 :      11988 :                                                                                                 value);
    2662         [ +  + ]:      11988 :                                         if (cstate->csv_mode)
    2663                 :       1360 :                                                 CopyAttributeOutCSV(cstate, string,
    2664                 :       1360 :                                                                                         cstate->force_quote_flags[attnum - 1],
    2665                 :       1360 :                                                                                         list_length(cstate->attnumlist) == 1);
    2666                 :            :                                         else
    2667                 :      10628 :                                                 CopyAttributeOutText(cstate, string);
    2668                 :            :                                 }
    2669                 :      53908 :                         }
    2670                 :            :                         else
    2671                 :            :                         {
    2672                 :            :                                 bytea      *outputbytes;
    2673                 :            : 
    2674                 :         31 :                                 outputbytes = SendFunctionCall(&out_functions[attnum - 1],
    2675                 :         31 :                                                                                            value);
    2676                 :         31 :                                 CopySendInt32(cstate, VARSIZE(outputbytes) - VARHDRSZ);
    2677                 :         31 :                                 CopySendData(cstate, VARDATA(outputbytes),
    2678                 :         31 :                                                          VARSIZE(outputbytes) - VARHDRSZ);
    2679                 :            :                         }
    2680                 :            :                 }
    2681                 :      53957 :         }
    2682                 :            : 
    2683                 :            :         /*
    2684                 :            :          * Finish off the row: write it to the destination, and update the count.
    2685                 :            :          * However, if we're in the context of a writable external table, we let
    2686                 :            :          * the caller do it - send the data to its local external source (see
    2687                 :            :          * external_insert() ).
    2688                 :            :          */
    2689         [ +  + ]:       5958 :         if(cstate->copy_dest != COPY_EXTERNAL_SOURCE)
    2690                 :            :         {
    2691                 :       3139 :                 CopySendEndOfRow(cstate);
    2692                 :       3139 :                 cstate->processed++;
    2693                 :       3139 :         }
    2694                 :            : 
    2695                 :       5958 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
    2696                 :       5958 : }
