    2698                 :            : 
    2699                 :            : /*
    2700                 :            :  * CopyFromCreateDispatchCommand
    2701                 :            :  *
    2702                 :            :  * The COPY command that needs to get dispatched to the QE's isn't necessarily
    2703                 :            :  * the same command that arrived from the parser to the QD. For example, we
    2704                 :            :  * always change filename to STDIN, we may pre-evaluate constant values or
    2705                 :            :  * functions on the QD and send them to the QE with an extended column list.
    2706                 :            :  */
    2707                 :            : static int CopyFromCreateDispatchCommand(CopyState cstate,
    2708                 :            :                                                                                   StringInfo cdbcopy_cmd,
    2709                 :            :                                                                                   GpPolicy  *policy,
    2710                 :            :                                                                                   AttrNumber    num_phys_attrs,
    2711                 :            :                                                                                   AttrNumber    num_defaults,
    2712                 :            :                                                                                   AttrNumber    p_nattrs,
    2713                 :            :                                                                                   AttrNumber    h_attnum,
    2714                 :            :                                                                                   int *defmap,
    2715                 :            :                                                                                   ExprState **defexprs,
    2716                 :            :                                                                                   Form_pg_attribute *attr)
    2717                 :            : {
    2718                 :            :         ListCell   *cur;
    2719                 :            :         bool            is_first_col;
    2720                 :            :         int                     i,
    2721                 :        355 :                                 p_index = 0;
    2722                 :        355 :         AttrNumber      extra_attr_count = 0; /* count extra attributes we add in the dispatcher COPY
    2723                 :            :                                                                                  usually non constant defaults we pre-evaluate in here */
    2724                 :            : 
    2725 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        355 :         Assert(Gp_role == GP_ROLE_DISPATCH);
    2726                 :            : 
    2727                 :            :         /* append schema and tablename */
    2728                 :        355 :         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, "COPY %s.%s",
    2729                 :        355 :                                          quote_identifier(get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(cstate->rel))),
    2730                 :        355 :                                          quote_identifier(RelationGetRelationName(cstate->rel)));
    2731                 :            :         /*
    2732                 :            :          * append column list. NOTE: if not specified originally, attnumlist will
    2733                 :            :          * include all non-dropped columns of the table by default
    2734                 :            :          */
    2735         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if(num_phys_attrs > 0) /* don't append anything for zero column table */
    2736                 :            :         {
    2737                 :        351 :                 is_first_col = true;
    2738         [ +  + ]:       2364 :                 foreach(cur, cstate->attnumlist)
    2739                 :            :                 {
    2740                 :       2013 :                         int                     attnum = lfirst_int(cur);
    2741                 :       2013 :                         int                     m = attnum - 1;
    2742                 :            : 
    2743                 :            :                         /* We don't add dropped attributes */
    2744         [ -  + ]:       2013 :                         if (attr[m]->attisdropped)
    2745                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    2746                 :            : 
    2747                 :            :                         /* append column string. quote it if needed */
    2748                 :       2013 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, (is_first_col ? "(%s" : ",%s"),
    2749                 :       2013 :                                                          quote_identifier(NameStr(attr[m]->attname)));
    2750                 :            : 
    2751                 :       2013 :                         is_first_col = false;
    2752                 :       2013 :                 }
    2753                 :            : 
    2754                 :            :                 /*
    2755                 :            :                  * In order to maintain consistency between the primary and mirror segment data, we
    2756                 :            :                  * want to evaluate all table columns that are not participating in this COPY command
    2757                 :            :                  * and have a non-constant default values on the dispatcher. If we let them evaluate
    2758                 :            :                  * on the primary and mirror executors separately - they will get different values.
    2759                 :            :                  * Also, if the distribution column is not participating and it has any default value,
    2760                 :            :                  * we have to evaluate it on the dispatcher only too, so that it wouldn't hash as a null
    2761                 :            :                  * and inserted as a default value on the segment databases.
    2762                 :            :                  *
    2763                 :            :                  * Therefore, we include these columns in the column list for the executor COPY.
    2764                 :            :                  * The default values will be evaluated on the dispatcher COPY and the results for
    2765                 :            :                  * the added columns will be appended to each data row that is shipped to the segments.
    2766                 :            :                  */
    2767                 :        351 :                 extra_attr_count = 0;
    2768                 :            : 
    2769         [ +  + ]:        369 :                 for (i = 0; i < num_defaults; i++)
    2770                 :            :                 {
    2771                 :         18 :                         bool add_to_list = false;
    2772                 :            : 
    2773                 :            :                         /* check 1: is this default for a distribution column? */
    2774         [ +  + ]:         36 :                         for (p_index = 0; p_index < p_nattrs; p_index++)
    2775                 :            :                         {
    2776                 :         18 :                                 h_attnum = policy->attrs[p_index];
    2777                 :            : 
    2778         [ +  + ]:         18 :                                 if(h_attnum - 1 == defmap[i])
    2779                 :          7 :                                         add_to_list = true;
    2780                 :         18 :                         }
    2781                 :            : 
    2782                 :            :                         /* check 2: is this a non constant default? */
    2783         [ +  + ]:         18 :                         if(defexprs[i]->expr->type != T_Const)
    2784                 :         14 :                                 add_to_list = true;
    2785                 :            : 
    2786         [ +  + ]:         18 :                         if(add_to_list)
    2787                 :            :                         {
    2788                 :            :                                 /* We don't add dropped attributes */
    2789                 :            :                                 /* XXXX: this check seems unnecessary given how CopyFromDispatch constructs defmap */
    2790         [ -  + ]:         15 :                                 if (attr[defmap[i]]->attisdropped)
    2791                 :          0 :                                         continue;
    2792                 :            : 
    2793                 :            :                                 /* append column string. quote it if needed */
    2794                 :         15 :                                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, (is_first_col ? "(%s" : ",%s"),
    2795                 :         15 :                                                                  quote_identifier(NameStr(attr[defmap[i]]->attname)));
    2796                 :            : 
    2797                 :         15 :                                 extra_attr_count++;
    2798                 :         15 :                                 is_first_col = false;
    2799                 :         15 :                         }
    2800                 :         18 :                 }
    2801                 :            : 
    2802         [ +  - ]:        351 :                 if (!is_first_col)
    2803                 :        351 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, ")");
    2804                 :        351 :         }
    2805                 :            : 
    2806                 :            :         /*
    2807                 :            :          * NOTE: we used to always pass STDIN here to the QEs. But since we want
    2808                 :            :          * the QEs to know the original file name for recording it in an error log file
    2809                 :            :          * (if they use one) we actually pass the filename here, and in the QE COPY
    2810                 :            :          * we get it, save it, and then always revert back to actually using STDIN.
    2811                 :            :          * (if we originally use STDIN we just pass it along and record that in the
    2812                 :            :          * error log file).
    2813                 :            :          */
    2814         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if(cstate->filename)
    2815                 :         96 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " FROM %s WITH", quote_literal_internal(cstate->filename));
    2816                 :            :         else
    2817                 :        259 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " FROM STDIN WITH");
    2818                 :            : 
    2819         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if (cstate->oids)
    2820                 :          3 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " OIDS");
    2821                 :            : 
    2822         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if (cstate->binary)
    2823                 :            :         {
    2824                 :          1 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " BINARY");
    2825                 :          1 :         }
    2826                 :            :         else
    2827                 :            :         {
    2828                 :        354 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " DELIMITER AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->delim));
    2829                 :        354 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " NULL AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->null_print));
    2830                 :            : 
    2831                 :            :                 /* if default escape in text format ("\") leave expression out */
    2832 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:        354 :                 if (!cstate->csv_mode && strcmp(cstate->escape, "\\") != 0)
    2833                 :          2 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " ESCAPE AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->escape));
    2834                 :            : 
    2835                 :            :                 /* if EOL is already defined it means that NEWLINE was declared. pass it along */
    2836         [ +  + ]:        354 :                 if (cstate->eol_type != EOL_UNKNOWN)
    2837                 :            :                 {
    2838 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:          5 :                         Assert(cstate->eol_str);
    2839                 :          5 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " NEWLINE AS '%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->eol_str));
    2840                 :          5 :                 }
    2841                 :            : 
    2842         [ +  + ]:        354 :                 if (cstate->csv_mode)
    2843                 :            :                 {
    2844                 :         46 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " CSV");
    2845                 :         46 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " QUOTE AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->quote));
    2846                 :         46 :                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " ESCAPE AS E'%s'", escape_quotes(cstate->escape));
    2847                 :            : 
    2848         [ +  + ]:         46 :                         if(cstate->force_notnull)
    2849                 :            :                         {
    2850                 :            :                                 ListCell   *l;
    2851                 :            : 
    2852                 :          3 :                                 is_first_col = true;
    2853                 :          3 :                                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " FORCE NOT NULL");
    2854                 :            : 
    2855         [ +  + ]:          7 :                                 foreach(l, cstate->force_notnull)
    2856                 :            :                                 {
    2857                 :          4 :                                         const char         *col_name = strVal(lfirst(l));
    2858                 :            : 
    2859                 :          4 :                                         appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, (is_first_col ? " %s" : ",%s"),
    2860                 :          4 :                                                                          quote_identifier(col_name));
    2861                 :          4 :                                         is_first_col = false;
    2862                 :          4 :                                 }
    2863                 :          3 :                         }
    2864                 :            :                         /* do NOT include HEADER. Header row is "swallowed" by dispatcher COPY */
    2865                 :         46 :                 }
    2866                 :            :         }
    2867                 :            : 
    2868         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if (cstate->fill_missing)
    2869                 :         11 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " FILL MISSING FIELDS");
    2870                 :            : 
    2871                 :            :         /* add single row error handling clauses if necessary */
    2872         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if (cstate->errMode != ALL_OR_NOTHING)
    2873                 :            :         {
    2874         [ +  + ]:         23 :                 if (cstate->errMode == SREH_LOG)
    2875                 :            :                 {
    2876                 :         14 :                         appendStringInfoString(cdbcopy_cmd, " LOG ERRORS");
    2877                 :         14 :                 }
    2878                 :            : 
    2879                 :         23 :                 appendStringInfo(cdbcopy_cmd, " SEGMENT REJECT LIMIT %d %s",
    2880                 :         23 :                                                  cstate->cdbsreh->rejectlimit, (cstate->cdbsreh->is_limit_in_rows ? "ROWS" : "PERCENT"));
    2881                 :         23 :         }
    2882                 :            : 
    2883                 :        355 :         return extra_attr_count;
    2884                 :            : }
    2885                 :            : 
    2886                 :            : /*
    2887                 :            :  * Copy FROM file to relation.
    2888                 :            :  */
    2889                 :            : void
    2890                 :            : CopyFromDispatch(CopyState cstate)
    2891                 :            : {
    2892                 :            :         TupleDesc       tupDesc;
    2893                 :            :         Form_pg_attribute *attr;
    2894                 :            :         AttrNumber      num_phys_attrs,
    2895                 :            :                                 attr_count,
    2896                 :            :                                 num_defaults;
    2897                 :            :         FmgrInfo   *in_functions;
    2898                 :            :         FmgrInfo        oid_in_function;
    2899                 :            :         FmgrInfo   *out_functions; /* for handling defaults in Greenplum Database */
    2900                 :            :         Oid                *typioparams;
    2901                 :            :         Oid                     oid_typioparam;
    2902                 :            :         int                     attnum;
    2903                 :            :         int                     i;
    2904                 :            :         int                     p_index;
    2905                 :            :         Oid                     in_func_oid;
    2906                 :            :         Oid                     out_func_oid;
    2907                 :            :         Datum      *values;
    2908                 :            :         bool       *nulls;
    2909                 :            :         int                *attr_offsets;
    2910                 :        355 :         int                     total_rejected_from_qes = 0;
    2911                 :            :         bool            isnull;
    2912                 :            :         bool       *isvarlena;
    2913                 :            :         ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo;
    2914                 :        355 :         EState     *estate = CreateExecutorState(); /* for ExecConstraints() */
    2915                 :        355 :         bool            file_has_oids = false;
    2916                 :            :         int                *defmap;
    2917                 :            :         ExprState **defexprs;           /* array of default att expressions */
    2918                 :            :         ExprContext *econtext;          /* used for ExecEvalExpr for default atts */
    2919                 :        355 :         MemoryContext oldcontext = CurrentMemoryContext;
    2920                 :            :         ErrorContextCallback errcontext;
    2921                 :        355 :         bool            no_more_data = false;
    2922                 :            :         ListCell   *cur;
    2923                 :        355 :         bool            cur_row_rejected = false;
    2924                 :            :         CdbCopy    *cdbCopy;
    2925                 :            : 
    2926                 :            :         /*
    2927                 :            :          * This stringInfo will contain 2 types of error messages:
    2928                 :            :          *
    2929                 :            :          * 1) Data errors refer to errors that are a result of inappropriate
    2930                 :            :          *        input data or constraint violations. All data error messages
    2931                 :            :          *        from the segment databases will be added to this variable and
    2932                 :            :          *        reported back to the client at the end of the copy command
    2933                 :            :          *        execution on the dispatcher.
    2934                 :            :          * 2) Any command execution error that occurs during this COPY session.
    2935                 :            :          *        Such errors will usually be failure to send data over the network,
    2936                 :            :          *        a COPY command that was rejected by the segment databases or any I/O
    2937                 :            :          *        error.
    2938                 :            :          */
    2939                 :            :         StringInfoData cdbcopy_err;
    2940                 :            : 
    2941                 :            :         /*
    2942                 :            :          * a reconstructed and modified COPY command that is dispatched to segments.
    2943                 :            :          */
    2944                 :            :         StringInfoData cdbcopy_cmd;
    2945                 :            : 
    2946                 :            :         /*
    2947                 :            :          * Variables for cdbpolicy
    2948                 :            :          */
    2949                 :            :         GpPolicy  *policy; /* the partitioning policy for this table */
    2950                 :            :         AttrNumber      p_nattrs; /* num of attributes in the distribution policy */
    2951                 :            :         Oid       *p_attr_types;        /* types for each policy attribute */
    2952                 :            : 
    2953                 :            :         /* variables for partitioning */
    2954                 :        355 :         Datum      *part_values = NULL;
    2955                 :        355 :         Oid                *part_attr_types = NULL; /* types for partitioning */
    2956                 :        355 :         Oid                *part_typio = NULL;
    2957                 :        355 :         FmgrInfo   *part_infuncs = NULL;
    2958                 :        355 :         AttrNumber *part_attnum = NULL;

    2971                 :            :         /*
    2972                 :            :          * In the case of partitioned tables with children that have different
    2973                 :            :          * distribution policies, we maintain a hash table of CdbHashs and
    2974                 :            :          * GpPolicies for each child table. We lazily add them to the hash --
    2975                 :            :          * when a partition is returned which we haven't seen before, we makeCdbHash
    2976                 :            :          * and copy the policy over.
    2977                 :            :          */

    3285                 :            : 
    3286                 :            :         /*
    3287                 :            :          * Dispatch the COPY command.
    3288                 :            :          *
    3289                 :            :          * From this point in the code we need to be extra careful about error
    3290                 :            :          * handling. ereport() must not be called until the COPY command sessions
    3291                 :            :          * are closed on the executors. Calling ereport() will leave the executors
    3292                 :            :          * hanging in COPY state.
    3293                 :            :          *
    3294                 :            :          * For errors detected by the dispatcher, we save the error message in
    3295                 :            :          * cdbcopy_err StringInfo, move on to closing all COPY sessions on the
    3296                 :            :          * executors and only then raise an error. We need to make sure to TRY/CATCH
    3297                 :            :          * all other errors that may be raised from elsewhere in the backend. All
    3298                 :            :          * error during COPY on the executors will be detected only when we end the
    3299                 :            :          * COPY session there, so we are fine there.
    3300                 :            :          */
    3301                 :        355 :         elog(DEBUG5, "COPY command sent to segdbs: %s", cdbcopy_cmd.data);
    3302         [ +  - ]:        355 :         PG_TRY();
    3303                 :            :         {
    3304                 :        355 :                 cdbCopyStart(cdbCopy, cdbcopy_cmd.data);
    3305                 :            :         }
    3306                 :        355 :         PG_CATCH();
    3307                 :            :         {
    3308                 :            :                 /* get error message from CopyStart */
    3309                 :          0 :                 appendBinaryStringInfo(&cdbcopy_err, cdbCopy->err_msg.data, cdbCopy->err_msg.len);
    3310                 :            : 
    3311                 :            :                 /* end COPY in all the segdbs in progress */
    3312                 :          0 :                 cdbCopyEnd(cdbCopy);
    3313                 :            : 
    3314                 :            :                 /* get error message from CopyEnd */
    3315                 :          0 :                 appendBinaryStringInfo(&cdbcopy_err, cdbCopy->err_msg.data, cdbCopy->err_msg.len);
    3316                 :            : 
    3317         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    3318                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_CDB_INTERNAL_ERROR),
    3319                 :            :                                  errmsg("%s", cdbcopy_err.data)));
    3320                 :          0 :                 PG_RE_THROW();
    3321                 :            :         }
    3322                 :        355 :         PG_END_TRY();
    3323                 :            : 
    3324                 :            :         /* Prepare to catch AFTER triggers. */
    3325                 :            :         //AfterTriggerBeginQuery();
    3326                 :            : 
    3327                 :            :         /*
    3328                 :            :          * Check BEFORE STATEMENT insertion triggers. It's debateable whether we
    3329                 :            :          * should do this for COPY, since it's not really an "INSERT" statement as
    3330                 :            :          * such. However, executing these triggers maintains consistency with the
    3331                 :            :          * EACH ROW triggers that we already fire on COPY.
    3332                 :            :          */
    3333                 :            :         //ExecBSInsertTriggers(estate, resultRelInfo);
    3334                 :            : 
    3335         [ +  + ]:        355 :         if (!cstate->binary)
    3336                 :        354 :                 file_has_oids = cstate->oids;        /* must rely on user to tell us... */
    3337                 :            :         else
    3338                 :            :         {
    3339                 :            :                 /* Read and verify binary header */
    3340                 :            :                 char            readSig[11];
    3341                 :            :                 int32           tmp_flags, tmp_extension;
    3342                 :            :                 int32           tmp;
    3343                 :            : 
    3344                 :            :                 /* Signature */
    3345 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:          1 :                 if (CopyGetData(cstate, readSig, 11) != 11 ||
    3346                 :          1 :                         memcmp(readSig, BinarySignature, 11) != 0)
    3347         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    3348                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),
    3349                 :            :                                          errmsg("COPY file signature not recognized")));
    3350                 :            :                 /* Flags field */
    3351         [ -  + ]:          1 :                 if (!CopyGetInt32(cstate, &tmp_flags))
    3352         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    3353                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),
    3354                 :            :                                          errmsg("invalid COPY file header (missing flags)")));
    3355                 :          1 :                 file_has_oids = (tmp_flags & (1 << 16)) != 0;
    3356                 :          1 :                 tmp = tmp_flags & ~(1 << 16);
    3357         [ -  + ]:          1 :                 if ((tmp >> 16) != 0)
    3358         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    3359                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),
    3360                 :            :                                  errmsg("unrecognized critical flags in COPY file header")));
    3361                 :            :                 /* Header extension length */
    3362 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:          1 :                 if (!CopyGetInt32(cstate, &tmp_extension) ||
    3363                 :          1 :                         tmp_extension < 0)
    3364         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
    3365                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_BAD_COPY_FILE_FORMAT),

    3990                 :            :                                 /*

    4119                 :         24 :         }
    4120                 :            : 
    4121                 :            :         /*
    4122                 :            :          * report all accumulated errors back to the client. We get here if an error
    4123                 :            :          * happened in all-or-nothing error handling mode or if reject limit was
    4124                 :            :          * reached in single-row error handling mode.
    4125                 :            :          */
    4126         [ +  + ]:        326 :         if (cdbCopy->remote_data_err)

    4130         [ -  + ]:        302 :         if (cdbCopy->io_errors)
    4131         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    4132                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_IO_ERROR),

    4134                 :            : 
    4135                 :            :         /*
    4136                 :            :          * switch back away from COPY error context callback. don't want line
    4137                 :            :          * error information anymore

    4139                 :        302 :         error_context_stack = errcontext.previous;
    4140                 :            : 
    4141                 :            :         /*
    4142                 :            :          * If we got here it means that either all the data was loaded or some rows
    4143                 :            :          * were rejected in SREH mode. In other words - nothing caused an abort.
    4144                 :            :          * We now want to report the actual number of rows loaded and rejected.
    4145                 :            :          * If any rows were rejected from the QE COPY processes subtract this number
    4146                 :            :          * from the number of rows that were successfully processed on the QD COPY
    4147                 :            :          * so that we can report the correct number.
    4148                 :            :          */
    4149         [ +  + ]:        302 :         if(cstate->cdbsreh)

    4153                 :            : 
    4154                 :            :                 /*
    4155                 :            :                  * If error log has been requested, then we send the row to the segment
    4156                 :            :                  * so that it can be written in the error log file. The segment process
    4157                 :            :                  * counts it again as a rejected row. So we ignore the reject count
    4158                 :            :                  * from the master and only consider the reject count from segments.
    4159                 :            :                  */
    4160         [ +  + ]:         14 :                 if (cstate->cdbsreh->log_to_file)
    4161                 :          9 :                         total_rejected_from_qd = 0;
    4162                 :            : 
    4163                 :         14 :                 total_rejected = total_rejected_from_qd + total_rejected_from_qes;
    4164                 :         14 :                 cstate->processed -= total_rejected;
    4165                 :            : 
    4166                 :            :                 /* emit a NOTICE with number of rejected rows */
    4167                 :         14 :                 ReportSrehResults(cstate->cdbsreh, total_rejected);
    4168                 :         14 :         }
    4169                 :            : 
    4170                 :            :

    4172                 :            :          * Done, clean up

    4307                 :            : 
    4308                 :            :         /*----------
    4309                 :            :          * Check to see if we can avoid writing WAL
    4310                 :            :          *
    4311                 :            :          * If archive logging is not enabled *and* either
    4312                 :            :          *      - table was created in same transaction as this COPY
    4313                 :            :          *      - data is being written to relfilenode created in this transaction
    4314                 :            :          * then we can skip writing WAL.  It's safe because if the transaction
    4315                 :            :          * doesn't commit, we'll discard the table (or the new relfilenode file).
    4316                 :            :          * If it does commit, we'll have done the heap_sync at the bottom of this
    4317                 :            :          * routine first.
    4318                 :            :          *
    4319                 :            :          * As mentioned in comments in utils/rel.h, the in-same-transaction test
    4320                 :            :          * is not completely reliable, since in rare cases rd_createSubid or
    4321                 :            :          * rd_newRelfilenodeSubid can be cleared before the end of the transaction.
    4322                 :            :          * However this is OK since at worst we will fail to make the optimization.
    4323                 :            :          *
    4324                 :            :          * Also, if the target file is new-in-transaction, we assume that checking
    4325                 :            :          * FSM for free space is a waste of time, even if we must use WAL because
    4326                 :            :          * of archiving.  This could possibly be wrong, but it's unlikely.
    4327                 :            :          *
    4328                 :            :          * The comments for heap_insert and RelationGetBufferForTuple specify that
    4329                 :            :          * skipping WAL logging is only safe if we ensure that our tuples do not
    4330                 :            :          * go into pages containing tuples from any other transactions --- but this
    4331                 :            :          * must be the case if we have a new table or new relfilenode, so we need
    4332                 :            :          * no additional work to enforce that.
    4333                 :            :          *----------
    4334                 :            :          */
    4335 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:       1065 :         if (cstate->rel->rd_createSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId ||
    4336                 :        891 :                 cstate->rel->rd_newRelfilenodeSubid != InvalidSubTransactionId)
    4337                 :            :         {
    4338                 :        174 :                 use_fsm = false;
    4339         [ +  - ]:        174 :                 if (!XLogArchivingActive())
    4340                 :        174 :                         use_wal = false;
    4341                 :        174 :         }

    5414                 :            :                                         {
    5415                 :          0 :                                                 cstate->raw_buf_done = true;
    5416                 :          0 :                                         }
    5417                 :          0 :                                         return true;
    5418                 :            :                                 }
    5419                 :          0 :                         }
    5420                 :            : 
    5421                 :          0 :                         cstate->cr_in_prevbuf = false;
    5422                 :          0 :                 }
    5423                 :          0 :         }
    5424                 :            : 
    5425                 :            :         /*
    5426                 :            :          * (we need a loop so that if eol_ch is found, but we are in quotes,
    5427                 :            :          * we can search for the next eol_ch)
    5428                 :            :          */

    5430                 :            :         {
    5431                 :            :                 /* reached end of buffer */

    5433                 :            :                 {

    5436                 :            : 
    5437         [ +  + ]:       1248 :                         if (cstate->line_buf.len > 1)

    5440                 :            : 
    5441         [ -  + ]:       1243 :                                 if (*last_ch == '\r')
    5442                 :            :                                 {

    5445                 :          0 :                                 }
    5446                 :       1243 :                         }
    5447                 :            :

    5451                 :            :                 }

    5455                 :     806466 :                         bool            eol_found = true;
    5456                 :            :

    5472                 :            :

    5474                 :            : 
    5475                 :            :                                 /* update file line for error message */
    5476                 :            :

    5482                 :            :

    5615                 :            : /*
    5616                 :            :  * Detected the eol type by looking at the first data row.
    5617                 :            :  * Possible eol types are NL, CR, or CRNL. If eol type was
    5618                 :            :  * detected, it is set and a boolean true is returned to
    5619                 :            :  * indicated detection was successful. If the first data row
    5620                 :            :  * is longer than the input buffer, we return false and will
    5621                 :            :  * try again in the next buffer.
    5622                 :            :  */
    5623                 :            : static bool
    5624                 :            : DetectLineEnd(CopyState cstate, size_t bytesread  __attribute__((unused)))
    5625                 :            : {
    5626                 :       3132 :         int                     index = 0;
    5627                 :       3132 :         int                     lineno = 0;
    5628                 :            :         char            c;
    5629                 :       3132 :         char            quotec = '\0',
    5630                 :       3132 :                                 escapec = '\0';
    5631                 :       3132 :         bool            csv = false;
    5632                 :            :         
    5633                 :            :         /*
    5634                 :            :          * CSV special case. See MPP-7819.
    5635                 :            :          * 
    5636                 :            :          * this functions may change the in_quote value while processing.
    5637                 :            :          * this is ok as we need to keep state in case we don't find EOL
    5638                 :            :          * in this buffer and need to be called again to continue searching.
    5639                 :            :          * BUT if EOL *was* found we must reset to the state we had since 
    5640                 :            :          * we are about to reprocess this buffer again in CopyReadLineCSV
    5641                 :            :          * from the same starting point as we are in right now. 
    5642                 :            :          */
    5643                 :       3132 :         bool save_inquote = cstate->in_quote;
    5644                 :       3132 :         bool save_lastwas = cstate->last_was_esc;
    5645                 :            : 
    5646                 :            :         /* if user specified NEWLINE we should never be here */
    5647 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       3132 :         Assert(!cstate->eol_str);
    5648                 :            : 
    5649         [ +  + ]:       3132 :         if (cstate->quote)                                   /* CSV format */
    5650                 :            :         {
    5651                 :        136 :                 csv = true;
    5652                 :        136 :                 quotec = cstate->quote[0];
    5653                 :        136 :                 escapec = cstate->escape[0];
    5654                 :            :                 /* ignore special escape processing if it's the same as quotec */
    5655         [ +  + ]:        136 :                 if (quotec == escapec)
    5656                 :        122 :                         escapec = '\0';
    5657                 :        136 :         }
    5658                 :            : 
    5659         [ +  - ]:      65949 :         while (index < RAW_BUF_SIZE)
    5660                 :            :         {
    5661                 :      65949 :                 c = cstate->raw_buf[index];
    5662                 :            : 
    5663         [ +  + ]:      65949 :                 if (csv)

    5665 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:       8087 :                         if (cstate->in_quote && c == escapec)

    5671                 :       8087 :                 }

                 [ +  + ]

    5682                 :            : 
    5683                 :       3129 :                                 cstate->in_quote = save_inquote; /* see comment at declaration */
    5684                 :       3129 :                                 cstate->last_was_esc = save_lastwas;

    5694         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,

    5735                 :            : /*
    5736                 :            :  *      Return decimal value for a hexadecimal digit
    5737                 :            :  */
    5738                 :            : static int
    5739                 :            : GetDecimalFromHex(char hex)
    5740                 :            : {
    5741         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (isdigit((unsigned char) hex))
    5742                 :          0 :                 return hex - '0';
    5743                 :            :         else
    5744                 :          0 :                 return tolower((unsigned char) hex) - 'a' + 10;
    5745                 :          0 : }
    5746                 :            : 
    5747                 :            : /*
    5748                 :            :  * Read all TEXT attributes. Attributes are parsed from line_buf and
    5749                 :            :  * inserted (all at once) to attribute_buf, while saving pointers to
    5750                 :            :  * each attribute's starting position.
    5751                 :            :  *
    5752                 :            :  * When this routine finishes execution both the nulls array and
    5753                 :            :  * the attr_offsets array are updated. The attr_offsets will include
    5754                 :            :  * the offset from the beginning of the attribute array of which
    5755                 :            :  * each attribute begins. If a specific attribute is not used for this
    5756                 :            :  * COPY command (ommitted from the column list), a value of 0 will be assigned.
    5757                 :            :  * For example: for table foo(a,b,c,d,e) and COPY foo(a,b,e)
    5758                 :            :  * attr_offsets may look something like this after this routine
    5759                 :            :  * returns: [0,20,0,0,55]. That means that column "a" value starts
    5760                 :            :  * at byte offset 0, "b" in 20 and "e" in 55, in attribute_buf.
    5761                 :            :  *
    5762                 :            :  * In the attribute buffer (attribute_buf) each attribute
    5763                 :            :  * is terminated with a '\0', and therefore by using the attr_offsets
    5764                 :            :  * array we could point to a beginning of an attribute and have it
    5765                 :            :  * behave as a C string, much like previously done in COPY.
    5766                 :            :  *
    5767                 :            :  * Another aspect to improving performance is reducing the frequency
    5768                 :            :  * of data load into buffers. The original COPY read attribute code
    5769                 :            :  * loaded a character at a time. In here we try to load a chunk of data
    5770                 :            :  * at a time. Usually a chunk will include a full data row
    5771                 :            :  * (unless we have an escaped delim). That effectively reduces the number of
    5772                 :            :  * loads by a factor of number of bytes per row. This improves performance
    5773                 :            :  * greatly, unfortunately it add more complexity to the code.
    5774                 :            :  *
    5775                 :            :  * Global participants in parsing logic:
    5776                 :            :  *
    5777                 :            :  * line_buf.cursor -- an offset from beginning of the line buffer
    5778                 :            :  * that indicates where we are about to begin the next scan. Note that
    5779                 :            :  * if we have WITH OIDS or if we ran CopyExtractRowMetaData this cursor is
    5780                 :            :  * already shifted and is not in the beginning of line buf anymore.
    5781                 :            :  *
    5782                 :            :  * attribute_buf.cursor -- an offset from the beginning of the
    5783                 :            :  * attribute buffer that indicates where the current attribute begins.
    5784                 :            :  */
    5785                 :            : 
    5786                 :            : void
    5787                 :            : CopyReadAttributesText(CopyState cstate, bool * __restrict nulls,
    5788                 :            :                                            int * __restrict attr_offsets, int num_phys_attrs, Form_pg_attribute * __restrict attr)
    5789                 :            : {
    5790                 :    1231146 :         char            delimc = cstate->delim[0];           /* delimiter character */
    5791                 :    1231146 :         char            escapec = cstate->escape[0]; /* escape character    */
    5792                 :            :         char       *scan_start;         /* pointer to line buffer for scan start. */
    5793                 :            :         char       *scan_end;           /* pointer to line buffer where char was found */
    5794                 :            :         char       *stop;
    5795                 :            :         char       *scanner;
    5796                 :    1231146 :         int                     attr_pre_len = 0;/* attr raw len, before processing escapes */
    5797                 :    1231146 :         int                     attr_post_len = 0;/* current attr len after escaping */
    5798                 :            :         int                     m;                              /* attribute index being parsed */
    5799                 :            :         int                     bytes_remaining;/* num bytes remaining to be scanned in line
    5800                 :            :                                                                  * buf */
    5801                 :            :         int                     chunk_start;    /* offset to beginning of line chunk to load */
    5802                 :    1231146 :         int                     chunk_len = 0;  /* length of chunk of data to load to attr buf */
    5803                 :            :         int                     oct_val;                /* byte value for octal escapes */
    5804                 :            :         int                     hex_val;
    5805                 :    1231146 :         int                     attnum = 0;             /* attribute number being parsed */
    5806                 :    1231146 :         int                     attribute = 1;
    5807                 :    1231146 :         bool            saw_high_bit = false;
    5808                 :            :         ListCell   *cur;                        /* cursor to attribute list used for this COPY */
    5809                 :            : 
    5810                 :            :         /* init variables for attribute scan */
    5811                 :    1231146 :         RESET_ATTRBUF;
    5812                 :            : 
    5813                 :            :         /* cursor is now > 0 if we copy WITH OIDS */
    5814                 :    1231146 :         scan_start = cstate->line_buf.data + cstate->line_buf.cursor;
    5815                 :    1231146 :         chunk_start = cstate->line_buf.cursor;
    5816                 :            : 
    5817                 :    1231146 :         cur = list_head(cstate->attnumlist);
    5818                 :            : 
    5819                 :            :         /* check for zero column table case */
    5820         [ +  + ]:    1231146 :         if(num_phys_attrs > 0)
    5821                 :            :         {
    5822                 :    1231145 :                 attnum = lfirst_int(cur);
    5823                 :    1231145 :                 m = attnum - 1;
    5824                 :    1231145 :         }
    5825                 :            : 
    5826         [ +  + ]:    1231146 :         if (cstate->escape_off)
    5827                 :          4 :                 escapec = delimc;               /* look only for delimiters, escapes are
    5828                 :            :                                                                  * disabled */
    5829                 :            : 
    5830                 :            :         /* have a single column only and no delim specified? take the fast track */

    6049                 :            :                                                         {

    6136                 :            :         /*

    6152                 :            :          * high bit set, we check that the changed attribute text is still
    6153                 :            :          * valid WRT encoding. We run the check on all attributes since

    6163                 :          0 :                 }
    6164                 :          0 :         }
    6165                 :    1223779 : }
    6166                 :            : 
    6167                 :            : /*
    6168                 :            :  * Read all the attributes of the data line in CSV mode,
    6169                 :            :  * performing de-escaping as needed. Escaping does not follow the normal
    6170                 :            :  * PostgreSQL text mode, but instead "standard" (i.e. common) CSV usage.
    6171                 :            :  *
    6172                 :            :  * Quoted fields can span lines, in which case the line end is embedded
    6173                 :            :  * in the returned string.
    6174                 :            :  *
    6175                 :            :  * null_print is the null marker string.  Note that this is compared to
    6176                 :            :  * the pre-de-escaped input string (thus if it is quoted it is not a NULL).
    6177                 :            :  *----------
    6178                 :            :  */
    6179                 :            : void
    6180                 :            : CopyReadAttributesCSV(CopyState cstate, bool *nulls, int *attr_offsets,
    6181                 :            :                                           int num_phys_attrs, Form_pg_attribute *attr)
    6182                 :            : {
    6183                 :     188881 :         char            delimc = cstate->delim[0];
    6184                 :     188881 :         char            quotec = cstate->quote[0];
    6185                 :     188881 :         char            escapec = cstate->escape[0];
    6186                 :            :         char            c;
    6187                 :     188881 :         int                     start_cursor = cstate->line_buf.cursor;
    6188                 :     188881 :         int                     end_cursor = start_cursor;
    6189                 :     188881 :         int                     input_len = 0;
    6190                 :            :         int                     attnum;                 /* attribute number being parsed */
    6191                 :     188881 :         int                     m = 0;                  /* attribute index being parsed */
    6192                 :     188881 :         int                     attribute = 1;
    6193                 :     188881 :         bool            in_quote = false;
    6194                 :     188881 :         bool            saw_quote = false;
    6195                 :            :         ListCell   *cur;                        /* cursor to attribute list used for this COPY */
    6196                 :            : 
    6197                 :            :         /* init variables for attribute scan */
    6198                 :     188881 :         RESET_ATTRBUF;
    6199                 :            : 
    6200                 :     188881 :         cur = list_head(cstate->attnumlist);
    6201                 :            : 
    6202         [ +  + ]:     188881 :         if(num_phys_attrs > 0)
    6203                 :            :         {
    6204                 :     188880 :                 attnum = lfirst_int(cur);
    6205                 :     188880 :                 m = attnum - 1;
    6206                 :     188880 :         }
    6207                 :            : 
    6208                 :   72655514 :         for (;;)
    6209                 :            :         {
    6210                 :   82038645 :                 end_cursor = cstate->line_buf.cursor;
    6211                 :            : 
    6212                 :            :                 /* finished processing attributes in line */
    6213         [ +  + ]:   82038645 :                 if (cstate->line_buf.cursor >= cstate->line_buf.len - 1)
    6214                 :            :                 {
    6215                 :     188696 :                         input_len = end_cursor - start_cursor;
    6216                 :            : 
    6217         [ -  + ]:     188696 :                         if (cstate->eol_type == EOL_CRLF)
    6218                 :            :                         {
    6219                 :            :                                 /* ignore the leftover CR */
    6220                 :          0 :                                 input_len--;
    6221                 :          0 :                                 cstate->attribute_buf.data[cstate->attribute_buf.cursor - 1] = '\0';
    6222                 :          0 :                         }
    6223                 :            : 
    6224                 :            :                         /* check whether raw input matched null marker */
    6225         [ +  + ]:     188696 :                         if(num_phys_attrs > 0)
    6226                 :            :                         {
    6227 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     188695 :                                 if (!saw_quote && input_len == cstate->null_print_len &&
                 [ +  + ]
    6228                 :     188317 :                                         strncmp(&cstate->line_buf.data[start_cursor], cstate->null_print, input_len) == 0)
    6229                 :     188263 :                                         nulls[m] = true;
    6230                 :            :                                 else
    6231                 :        432 :                                         nulls[m] = false;
    6232                 :     188695 :                         }
    6233                 :            : 
    6234                 :            :                         /* if zero column table and data is trying to get in */

    6600                 :          0 :                                                 break;

    6645                 :            :                         {

    6648                 :            :                                  * prefer to dump these using the C-like notation, rather than
    6649                 :            :                                  * a backslash and the literal character, because it makes the
    6650                 :            :                                  * dump file a bit more proof against Microsoftish data
    6651                 :            :                                  * mangling.
    6652                 :            :                                  */
    6653   [ #  #  #  #  :          0 :                                 switch (c)
                #  #  # ]
    6654                 :            :                                 {
    6655                 :            :                                         case '\b':

    6725                 :       1386 :                 quotec = cstate->quote[0];

         [ +  + ][ +  + ]

    6760                 :         19 :                                         break;
    6761                 :            :                                 }

    6802                 :            : /*
    6803                 :            :  * CopyGetAttnums - build an integer list of attnums to be copied
    6804                 :            :  *
    6805                 :            :  * The input attnamelist is either the user-specified column list,
    6806                 :            :  * or NIL if there was none (in which case we want all the non-dropped
    6807                 :            :  * columns).

    6829                 :       3545 :         }

    6834                 :            :

    6839                 :            :                         int                     i;
    6840                 :            :

    6843         [ +  + ]:      44695 :                         for (i = 0; i < tupDesc->natts; i++)
    6844                 :            :                         {

    6846                 :         54 :                                         continue;

    6850                 :       8078 :                                         break;
    6851                 :            :                                 }

    6859                 :            :                                                    name, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));

    6865                 :          0 :                         }

    6879                 :            : #define COPY_FIND_MD_DELIM \
    6880                 :            : md_delim = memchr(line_start, COPY_METADATA_DELIM, Min(32, cstate->line_buf.len)); \

    6891                 :            : /*
    6892                 :            :  * CopyExtractRowMetaData - extract embedded row number from data.
    6893                 :            :  *
    6894                 :            :  * If data is being parsed in execute mode the parser (QE) doesn't
    6895                 :            :  * know the original line number (in the original file) of the current
    6896                 :            :  * row. Therefore the QD sends this information along with the data.
    6897                 :            :  * other metadata that the QD sends includes whether the data was
    6898                 :            :  * converted to server encoding (should always be the case, unless
    6899                 :            :  * encoding error happened and we're in error log mode).
    6900                 :            :  *
    6901                 :            :  * in:
    6902                 :            :  *    line_buf: <original_num>^<buf_converted>^<data for this row>
    6903                 :            :  *    lineno: ?
    6904                 :            :  *    line_buf_converted: ?
    6905                 :            :  *
    6906                 :            :  * out:
    6907                 :            :  *    line_buf: <data for this row>
    6908                 :            :  *    lineno: <original_num>
    6909                 :            :  *    line_buf_converted: <t/f>
    6910                 :            :  */
    6911                 :            : static
    6912                 :            : void CopyExtractRowMetaData(CopyState cstate)
    6913                 :            : {
    6914                 :     607835 :         char *md_delim = NULL; /* position of the metadata delimiter */
    6915                 :            :         
    6916                 :            :         /*
    6917                 :            :          * Line_buf may have already skipped an OID column if WITH OIDS defined,
    6918                 :            :          * so we need to start from cursor not always from beginning of linebuf.
    6919                 :            :          */
    6920                 :     607835 :         char *line_start = cstate->line_buf.data + cstate->line_buf.cursor;
    6921                 :     607835 :         int  value_len = 0;
    6922                 :            : 
    6923                 :     607835 :         cstate->md_error = false;
    6924                 :            :         
    6925                 :            :         /* look for the first delimiter, and extract lineno */
    6926 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     607835 :         COPY_FIND_MD_DELIM;
                 [ +  - ]
    6927                 :            :         
    6928                 :            :         /* 
    6929                 :            :          * make sure MD exists. that should always be the case
    6930                 :            :          * unless we run into an edge case - see MPP-8052. if that 
    6931                 :            :          * happens md_error is now set. we raise an error. 
    6932                 :            :          */
    6933         [ -  + ]:     607835 :         if(cstate->md_error)
    6934         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    6935                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
    6936                 :            :                                  errmsg("COPY metadata not found. This probably means that there is a "
    6937                 :            :                                                 "mixture of newline types in the data. Use the NEWLINE keyword "
    6938                 :            :                                                 "in order to resolve this reliably.")));
    6939                 :            : 
    6940                 :     607835 :         cstate->cur_lineno = atoi(line_start);
    6941                 :            : 
    6942                 :     607835 :         *md_delim = COPY_METADATA_DELIM; /* restore the line_buf byte after setting it to \0 */
    6943                 :            : 
    6944                 :            :         /* reposition line buf cursor to see next metadata value (skip lineno) */
    6945                 :     607835 :         cstate->line_buf.cursor += value_len;
    6946                 :     607835 :         line_start = cstate->line_buf.data + cstate->line_buf.cursor;
    6947                 :            : 
    6948                 :            :         /* look for the second delimiter, and extract line_buf_converted */
    6949 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:     607835 :         COPY_FIND_MD_DELIM;
                 [ +  - ]
    6950 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:     607835 :         Assert(*line_start == '0' || *line_start == '1'); 
                 [ -  + ]
    6951                 :     607835 :         cstate->line_buf_converted = atoi(line_start);
    6952                 :            :         
    6953                 :     607835 :         *md_delim = COPY_METADATA_DELIM;
    6954                 :     607835 :         cstate->line_buf.cursor += value_len;
    6955                 :     607835 : }

    6957                 :            : /*
    6958                 :            :  * error context callback for COPY FROM
    6959                 :            :  */
    6960                 :            : static void
    6961                 :            : copy_in_error_callback(void *arg)
    6962                 :            : {
    6963                 :        182 :         CopyState       cstate = (CopyState) arg;
    6964                 :            :         char buffer[20];
    6965                 :            : 
    6966                 :            :         /*
    6967                 :            :          * If we saved the error context from a QE in cdbcopy.c append it here.
    6968                 :            :          */

    6972                 :         24 :                 return;
    6973                 :            :         }
    6974                 :            : 
    6975                 :            :         /* don't need to print out context if error wasn't local */
    6976         [ -  + ]:        158 :         if (cstate->error_on_executor)
    6977                 :          0 :                 return;
    6978                 :            : 
    6979         [ -  + ]:        158 :         if (cstate->binary)
    6980                 :            :         {
    6981                 :            :                 /* can't usefully display the data */
    6982         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (cstate->cur_attname)
    6983                 :          0 :                         errcontext("COPY %s, line %s, column %s",
    6984                 :          0 :                                            cstate->cur_relname,
    6985                 :          0 :                                            linenumber_atoi(buffer, cstate->cur_lineno),
    6986                 :          0 :                                            cstate->cur_attname);
    6987                 :            :                 else
    6988                 :          0 :                         errcontext("COPY %s, line %s",
    6989                 :          0 :                                            cstate->cur_relname,
    6990                 :          0 :                                            linenumber_atoi(buffer, cstate->cur_lineno));
    6991                 :          0 :         }
    6992                 :            :         else
    6993                 :            :         {
    6994         [ +  + ]:        158 :         if (cstate->cur_attname)
    6995                 :            :         {
    6996                 :            :                 /* error is relevant to a particular column */
    6997                 :            :                 char       *att_buf;
    6998                 :            : 
    6999                 :         60 :                 att_buf = limit_printout_length(cstate->attribute_buf.data);
    7000                 :            : 
    7001                 :         60 :                 errcontext("COPY %s, line %s, column %s",
    7002                 :         60 :                                    cstate->cur_relname,
    7003                 :         60 :                                    linenumber_atoi(buffer, cstate->cur_lineno),
    7004                 :         60 :                                    att_buf);
    7005                 :         60 :                 pfree(att_buf);
    7006                 :         60 :         }
    7007                 :            :         else
    7008                 :            :         {
    7009                 :            :                 /* error is relevant to a particular line */
    7010 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:         98 :                 if (cstate->line_buf_converted || !cstate->need_transcoding)
    7011                 :            :                 {
    7012                 :            :                         char       *line_buf;
    7013                 :            : 
    7014                 :         98 :                         line_buf = extract_line_buf(cstate);
    7015                 :         98 :                         truncateEolStr(line_buf, cstate->eol_type);
    7016                 :            : 
    7017                 :         98 :                         errcontext("COPY %s, line %s: \"%s\"",
    7018                 :         98 :                                            cstate->cur_relname,
    7019                 :         98 :                                            linenumber_atoi(buffer, cstate->cur_lineno),
    7020                 :         98 :                                            line_buf);
    7021                 :         98 :                         pfree(line_buf);
    7022                 :         98 :                 }
    7023                 :            :                 else
    7024                 :            :                 {
    7025                 :            :                         /*
    7026                 :            :                          * Here, the line buffer is still in a foreign encoding,
    7027                 :            :                          * and indeed it's quite likely that the error is precisely
    7028                 :            :                          * a failure to do encoding conversion (ie, bad data).  We
    7029                 :            :                          * dare not try to convert it, and at present there's no way
    7030                 :            :                          * to regurgitate it without conversion.  So we have to punt
    7031                 :            :                          * and just report the line number.
    7032                 :            :                          */
    7033                 :          0 :                         errcontext("COPY %s, line %s",
    7034                 :          0 :                                            cstate->cur_relname,
