    7040                 :            : 
    7041                 :            : /*
    7042                 :            :  * If our (copy of) linebuf has the embedded original row number and other
    7043                 :            :  * row-specific metadata, remove it. It is not part of the actual data, and
    7044                 :            :  * should not be displayed.
    7045                 :            :  *
    7046                 :            :  * we skip this step, however, if md_error was previously set by
    7047                 :            :  * CopyExtractRowMetaData. That should rarely happen, though.
    7048                 :            :  *
    7049                 :            :  * Returned value is a palloc'ed string to print.  The caller should pfree it.
    7050                 :            :  */
    7051                 :            : static char *
    7052                 :            : extract_line_buf(CopyState cstate)
    7053                 :            : {
    7054                 :         98 :         char       *line_buf = cstate->line_buf.data;
    7055                 :            : 
    7056 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:         98 :         if (cstate->err_loc_type == ROWNUM_EMBEDDED && !cstate->md_error)
    7057                 :            :         {
    7058                 :            :                 /* the following is a compacted mod of CopyExtractRowMetaData */
    7059                 :         47 :                 int value_len = 0;
    7060                 :         47 :                 char *line_start = cstate->line_buf.data;
    7061                 :         47 :                 char *lineno_delim = memchr(line_start, COPY_METADATA_DELIM,
    7062         [ -  + ]:         47 :                                                                         Min(32, cstate->line_buf.len));
    7063                 :            : 
    7064 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:         47 :                 if (lineno_delim && (lineno_delim != line_start))
    7065                 :            :                 {
    7066                 :            :                         /*
    7067                 :            :                          * we only continue parsing metadata if the first extraction above
    7068                 :            :                          * succeeded. there are some edge cases where we may not have a line
    7069                 :            :                          * with MD to parse, for example if some non-copy related error
    7070                 :            :                          * propagated here and we don't yet have a proper data line.
    7071                 :            :                          * see MPP-11328
    7072                 :            :                          */
    7073                 :         47 :                         value_len = lineno_delim - line_start + 1;
    7074                 :         47 :                         line_start += value_len;
    7075                 :            : 
    7076                 :         47 :                         lineno_delim = memchr(line_start, COPY_METADATA_DELIM,
    7077         [ -  + ]:         47 :                                                                   Min(32, cstate->line_buf.len));
    7078                 :            : 
    7079                 :         47 :                         value_len = lineno_delim - line_start + 1;
    7080                 :         47 :                         line_start += value_len;
    7081                 :         47 :                         line_buf = line_start;
    7082                 :         47 :                 }
    7083                 :         47 :         }
    7084                 :            : 
    7085                 :            :         /*
    7086                 :            :          * Finally allocate a new buffer and trim the string to a reasonable
    7087                 :            :          * length.  We need a copy since this might be called from non-ERROR
    7088                 :            :          * context like NOTICE, and we should preserve the original.
    7089                 :            :          */
    7090                 :         98 :         return limit_printout_length(line_buf);
    7091                 :            : }
    7092                 :            : 
    7093                 :            : /*
    7094                 :            :  * Make sure we don't print an unreasonable amount of COPY data in a message.
    7095                 :            :  *
    7096                 :            :  * It would seem a lot easier to just use the sprintf "precision" limit to
    7097                 :            :  * truncate the string.  However, some versions of glibc have a bug/misfeature
    7098                 :            :  * that vsnprintf will always fail (return -1) if it is asked to truncate
    7099                 :            :  * a string that contains invalid byte sequences for the current encoding.
    7100                 :            :  * So, do our own truncation.  We return a pstrdup'd copy of the input.
    7101                 :            :  */
    7102                 :            : char *
    7103                 :            : limit_printout_length(const char *str)
    7104                 :            : {
    7105                 :            : #define MAX_COPY_DATA_DISPLAY 100
    7106                 :            : 
    7107                 :       7501 :         int                     slen = strlen(str);
    7108                 :            :         int                     len;
    7109                 :            :         char       *res;
    7110                 :            : 
    7111                 :            :         /* Fast path if definitely okay */
    7112         [ +  - ]:       7501 :         if (slen <= MAX_COPY_DATA_DISPLAY)
    7113                 :       7501 :                 return pstrdup(str);
    7114                 :            : 
    7115                 :            :         /* Apply encoding-dependent truncation */
    7116                 :          0 :         len = pg_mbcliplen(str, slen, MAX_COPY_DATA_DISPLAY);
    7117                 :            : 
    7118                 :            :         /*
    7119                 :            :          * Truncate, and add "..." to show we truncated the input.
    7120                 :            :          */
    7121                 :          0 :         res = (char *) palloc(len + 4);
    7122                 :          0 :         memcpy(res, str, len);
    7123                 :          0 :         strcpy(res + len, "...");
    7124                 :            : 
    7125                 :          0 :         return res;
    7126                 :       7501 : }
    7127                 :            : 
    7128                 :            : 
    7129                 :            : static void
    7130                 :            : attr_get_key(CopyState cstate, CdbCopy *cdbCopy, int original_lineno_for_qe,
    7131                 :            :                          unsigned int target_seg,
    7132                 :            :                          AttrNumber p_nattrs, AttrNumber *attrs,
    7133                 :            :                          Form_pg_attribute *attr_descs, int *attr_offsets, bool *attr_nulls,
    7134                 :            :                          FmgrInfo *in_functions, Oid *typioparams, Datum *values)
    7135                 :            : {
    7136                 :            :         AttrNumber p_index;
    7137                 :            : 
    7138                 :            :         /*
    7139                 :            :          * Since we only need the internal format of values that
    7140                 :            :          * we want to hash on (partitioning keys only), we want to
    7141                 :            :          * skip converting the other values so we can run faster.
    7142                 :            :          */

    7144                 :            :         {
    7145                 :            :                 ListCell *cur;
    7146                 :            : 
    7147                 :            :                 /*
    7148                 :            :                  * For this partitioning key, search for its location in the attr list.
    7149                 :            :                  * (note that fields may be out of order, so this is necessary).
    7150                 :            :                  */
    7151         [ +  + ]:     633756 :                 foreach(cur, cstate->attnumlist)
    7152                 :            :                 {
    7153                 :     631259 :                         int                     attnum = lfirst_int(cur);
    7154                 :     631259 :                         int                     m = attnum - 1;
    7155                 :            :                         char       *string;
    7156                 :            :                         bool            isnull;
    7157                 :            : 
    7158         [ +  + ]:     631259 :                         if (attnum == attrs[p_index])
    7159                 :            :                         {
    7160                 :     614580 :                                 string = cstate->attribute_buf.data + attr_offsets[m];
    7161                 :            : 
    7162         [ +  + ]:     614580 :                                 if (attr_nulls[m])
    7163                 :         43 :                                         isnull = true;
    7164                 :            :                                 else
    7165                 :     614537 :                                         isnull = false;
    7166                 :            : 
    7167 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:     614580 :                                 if (cstate->csv_mode && isnull &&
                 [ -  + ]
    7168                 :          2 :                                         cstate->force_notnull_flags[m])
    7169                 :            :                                 {
    7170                 :          0 :                                         string = cstate->null_print;         /* set to NULL string */
    7171                 :          0 :                                         isnull = false;
    7172                 :          0 :                                 }
    7173                 :            : 
    7174                 :            :                                 /* we read an SQL NULL, no need to do anything */
    7175         [ +  + ]:     614580 :                                 if (!isnull)
    7176                 :            :                                 {
    7177                 :     614537 :                                         cstate->cur_attname = NameStr(attr_descs[m]->attname);
    7178                 :            : 
    7179                 :     614537 :                                         values[m] = InputFunctionCall(&in_functions[m],

    7181                 :     614537 :                                                                                                   typioparams[m],
    7182                 :     614537 :                                                                                                   attr_descs[m]->atttypmod);
    7183                 :            : 
    7184                 :     614537 :                                         attr_nulls[m] = false;
    7185                 :     614537 :                                         cstate->cur_attname = NULL;
    7186                 :     614537 :                                 }               /* end if (!isnull) */
    7187                 :            :

    7189                 :            :                                                  * attribute */
    7190                 :            :                         }

    7194                 :            : 
    7195                 :            : /*
    7196                 :            :  * The following are custom versions of the string function strchr().
    7197                 :            :  * As opposed to the original strchr which searches through
    7198                 :            :  * a string until the target character is found, or a NULL is
    7199                 :            :  * found, this version will not return when a NULL is found.
    7200                 :            :  * Instead it will search through a pre-defined length of
    7201                 :            :  * bytes and will return only if the target character(s) is reached.
    7202                 :            :  *
    7203                 :            :  * If our client encoding is not a supported server encoding, we
    7204                 :            :  * know that it is not safe to look at each character as trailing
    7205                 :            :  * byte in a multibyte character may be a 7-bit ASCII equivalent.
    7206                 :            :  * Therefore we use pg_encoding_mblen to skip to the end of the
    7207                 :            :  * character.
    7208                 :            :  *
    7209                 :            :  * returns:
    7210                 :            :  *       pointer to c - if c is located within the string.
    7211                 :            :  *       NULL - if c was not found in specified length of search. Note:
    7212                 :            :  *                      this DOESN'T mean that a '\0' was reached.
    7213                 :            :  */
    7214                 :            : char *
    7215                 :            : scanTextLine(CopyState cstate, const char *s, char eol, size_t len)
    7216                 :            : {
    7217                 :            :                 
    7218 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:    1232470 :         if (cstate->encoding_embeds_ascii && !cstate->line_buf_converted)
    7219                 :            :         {
    7220                 :            :                 int                     mblen;
    7221                 :          0 :                 const char *end = s + len;
    7222                 :            :                 
    7223                 :            :                 /* we may need to skip the end of a multibyte char from the previous buffer */
    7224                 :          0 :                 s += cstate->missing_bytes;
    7225                 :            :                 
    7226                 :          0 :                 mblen = pg_encoding_mblen(cstate->client_encoding, s);
    7227                 :            : 
    7228 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 for (; *s != eol && s < end; s += mblen)
    7229                 :          0 :                         mblen = pg_encoding_mblen(cstate->client_encoding, s);
    7230                 :            : 
    7231                 :            :                 /* 
    7232                 :            :                  * MPP-10802
    7233                 :            :                  * if last char is a partial mb char (the rest of its bytes are in the next 
    7234                 :            :                  * buffer) save # of missing bytes for this char and skip them next time around 
    7235                 :            :                  */
    7236         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 cstate->missing_bytes = (s > end ? s - end : 0);
    7237                 :            :                         
    7238         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 return ((*s == eol) ? (char *) s : NULL);
    7239                 :            :         }
    7240                 :            :         else
    7241                 :    1232470 :                 return memchr(s, eol, len);
    7242                 :    1232470 : }

    7302                 :            : /* remove end of line chars from end of a buffer */

    7304                 :            : {

    7492                 :            : /*
    7493                 :            :  * CopyCheckIsLastLine
    7494                 :            :  *
    7495                 :            :  * This routine checks if the line being looked at is the last line of data.
    7496                 :            :  * If it is, it makes sure that this line is terminated with an EOL. We must
    7497                 :            :  * do this check in order to support files that don't end up EOL before EOF,
    7498                 :            :  * because we want to treat that last line as normal - and be able to pre
    7499                 :            :  * process it like the other lines (remove metadata chars, encoding conversion).
    7500                 :            :  *
    7501                 :            :  * See MPP-4406 for an example of why this is needed.
    7502                 :            :  *
    7503                 :            :  * Notice: if line_buf is empty, no need to add EOL
    7504                 :            :  */
    7505                 :            : static bool CopyCheckIsLastLine(CopyState cstate)
    7506                 :            : {
    7507 [ +  + ][ +  + ]:       2551 :         if (cstate->fe_eof && cstate->line_buf.len > 0)
    7508                 :            :         {
    7509                 :          2 :                 concatenateEol(cstate);
    7510                 :          2 :                 return true;
    7511                 :            :         }
    7512                 :            :         
    7513                 :       2549 :         return false;
    7514                 :       2551 : }
    7515                 :            : 
    7516                 :            : /*
    7517                 :            :  * setEncodingConversionProc
    7518                 :            :  *
    7519                 :            :  * COPY and External tables use a custom path to the encoding conversion
    7520                 :            :  * API because external tables have their own encoding (which is not
    7521                 :            :  * necessarily client_encoding). We therefore have to set the correct
    7522                 :            :  * encoding conversion function pointer ourselves, to be later used in
    7523                 :            :  * the conversion engine.
    7524                 :            :  *
    7525                 :            :  * The code here mimics a part of SetClientEncoding() in mbutils.c
    7526                 :            :  */
    7527                 :            : void setEncodingConversionProc(CopyState cstate, int client_encoding, bool iswritable)
    7528                 :            : {
    7529                 :            :         Oid             conversion_proc;
    7530                 :            :         
    7531                 :            :         /*
    7532                 :            :          * COPY FROM and RET: convert from client to server
    7533                 :            :          * COPY TO   and WET: convert from server to client
    7534                 :            :          */
    7535         [ +  + ]:       4870 :         if (iswritable)
    7536                 :        192 :                 conversion_proc = FindDefaultConversionProc(GetDatabaseEncoding(),
    7537                 :        192 :                                                                                                         client_encoding);
    7538                 :            :         else            
    7539                 :       4678 :                 conversion_proc = FindDefaultConversionProc(client_encoding,
    7540                 :       4678 :                                                                                                     GetDatabaseEncoding());

    7554                 :            : 
    7555                 :            : /*
    7556                 :            :  * preProcessDataLine
    7557                 :            :  *
    7558                 :            :  * When Done reading a complete data line set input row number for error report
    7559                 :            :  * purposes (this also removes any metadata that was concatenated to the data
    7560                 :            :  * by the QD during COPY) and convert it to server encoding if transcoding is
    7561                 :            :  * needed.
    7562                 :            :  */
    7563                 :            : static
    7564                 :            : void preProcessDataLine(CopyState cstate)
    7565                 :            : {
    7566                 :            :         char       *cvt;
    7567                 :    1420039 :         bool            force_transcoding = false;
    7568                 :            : 
    7569                 :            :         /*
    7570                 :            :          * Increment line count by 1 if we have access to all the original
    7571                 :            :          * data rows and can count them reliably (ROWNUM_ORIGINAL). However
    7572                 :            :          * if we have ROWNUM_EMBEDDED the original row number for this row
    7573                 :            :          * was sent to us with the data (courtesy of the data distributor), so
    7574                 :            :          * get that number instead.
    7575                 :            :          */
    7576         [ +  + ]:    1420039 :         if(cstate->err_loc_type == ROWNUM_ORIGINAL)
    7577                 :            :         {
    7578                 :     812256 :                 cstate->cur_lineno++;
    7579                 :     812256 :         }
    7580         [ +  - ]:     607783 :         else if(cstate->err_loc_type == ROWNUM_EMBEDDED)
    7581                 :            :         {
    7582 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     607783 :                 Assert(Gp_role == GP_ROLE_EXECUTE);
    7583                 :            :                 
    7584                 :            :                 /*
    7585                 :            :                  * Extract various metadata sent to us from the QD COPY about this row:
    7586                 :            :                  * 1) the original line number of the row.
    7587                 :            :                  * 2) if the row was converted to server encoding or not
    7588                 :            :                  */
    7589                 :     607783 :                 CopyExtractRowMetaData(cstate); /* sets cur_lineno internally */
    7590                 :            :                 
    7591                 :            :                 /* check if QD sent us a badly encoded row, still in client_encoding, 
    7592                 :            :                  * in order to catch the encoding error ourselves. if line_buf_converted
    7593                 :            :                  * is false after CopyExtractRowMetaData then we must transcode and catch
    7594                 :            :                  * the error. Verify that we are indeed in SREH error log mode. that's
    7595                 :            :                  * the only valid path for receiving an unconverted data row.
    7596                 :            :                  */
    7597         [ -  + ]:     607783 :                 if (!cstate->line_buf_converted)
    7598                 :            :                 {
    7599 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(cstate->errMode == SREH_LOG);
    7600                 :          0 :                         force_transcoding = true; 
    7601                 :          0 :                 }
    7602                 :            :                         
    7603                 :     607783 :         }
    7604                 :            :         else
    7605                 :            :         {
    7606         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(false); /* byte offset not yet supported */
    7607                 :            :         }
    7608                 :            :         
    7609 [ +  + ][ -  + ]:    1420039 :         if (cstate->need_transcoding || force_transcoding)
    7610                 :            :         {
    7611                 :     812256 :                 cvt = (char *) pg_custom_to_server(cstate->line_buf.data,
    7612                 :     812256 :                                                                                    cstate->line_buf.len,
    7613                 :     812256 :                                                                                    cstate->client_encoding,
    7614                 :     812256 :                                                                                    cstate->enc_conversion_proc);
    7615                 :            :                 
    7616 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:     812256 :                 Assert(!force_transcoding); /* if force is 't' we must have failed in the conversion */
    7617                 :            :                 
    7618         [ -  + ]:     812256 :                 if (cvt != cstate->line_buf.data)
    7619                 :            :                 {
    7620                 :            :                         /* transfer converted data back to line_buf */
    7621                 :          0 :                         RESET_LINEBUF;
    7622                 :          0 :                         appendBinaryStringInfo(&cstate->line_buf, cvt, strlen(cvt));
    7623                 :          0 :                         pfree(cvt);
    7624                 :          0 :                 }
    7625                 :     812256 :         }
    7626                 :            :         /* indicate that line buf is in server encoding */
    7627                 :    1420039 :         cstate->line_buf_converted = true;
    7628                 :    1420039 : }
